title: Acceptance Review: [epic] Fix monitoring-surfaced plugin defects
description:
## Description
Verify the epic outcome before closure.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All child implementation tasks closed
- [ ] Required tests/checks completed and passing
- [ ] No unresolved critical defects
- [ ] Follow-up issues filed for non-blocking gaps

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Acceptance review complete. All 3 epic success criteria PASS: (1) feedback.md Step 4 + apply/submit branches instruct Read-before-edit for file-backed content, complementary to the fileless case; (2) planning.md step 5 states the bd dep add same-type constraint + workaround; (3) analyze-sessions.py aliasing verified at runtime — html-ask cleared from the unmatched table, skill rows consolidated. tests/run-all.sh: 189 tests, 0 failures. No defects. Note: d9a's SKILL_RENAME_ALIASES is additive (14 entries covering other historical renames) — legitimate, not scope creep.

title: beads-plan: surface bd dep add same-type constraint in planning.md
description:
## Description

beads-plan and beads-work agents run `bd dep add` across issue types and fail with `Error: epics can only block other epics, not tasks` (or the task equivalent). Seen in 7 beads-plan episodes and 1 beads-work episode.

Root cause: the same-type constraint IS documented in `beads-core/references/beads-issue-workflow.md` (~line 64), but `beads-plan/SKILL.md` step 3 loads that doc only conditionally ("For label and dependency rules, load..."). Agents who think they know `bd dep add` skip the load and never see the rule. `planning.md` step 5 mentions `bd dep add` with no type constraint.

## Instructions

1. In `beads-core/references/planning.md`, at the `bd dep add` step (step 5 / ~line 11), inline the constraint: block edges connect same-type issues only — epic<->epic, task<->task; to order a task against an epic, depend on a task inside it.
2. Scan the beads-work path (`beads-core/references/work-intake.md`, `execution-orchestration.md`) for any `bd dep add` guidance with the same gap; add the same one-line constraint where dependencies are created.

## Files to Modify

- `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/references/planning.md`
- `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/references/execution-orchestration.md` (if it directs dep creation)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] planning.md states the epic<->epic / task<->task constraint at the dependency-creation step
- [ ] The beads-work reference path carries the same constraint wherever it directs `bd dep add`
- [ ] The guidance names the cross-type workaround (depend on a task inside the epic)

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Fixed. planning.md step 5 now states the same-type dependency constraint (epic↔epic, task↔task) and the cross-type workaround. work-intake.md/execution-orchestration.md direct no bd dep add — no gap there.

title: html-feedback: Read source file before editing it
description:
## Description

When html-feedback applies user comments back to the underlying source content, it edits a file the agent never opened in-session. The first Edit/Write fails with `File has not been read yet. Read it first before writing to it.` Confirmed in 3 of 4 sliced html-feedback episodes (identical error) plus a retry in a 4th.

Root cause: the failure is in the reference doc, not SKILL.md. `feedback.md` Step 4 says "Apply every comment to the underlying source of the content" with no instruction to Read that source first. The `action: "apply"` and `action: "submit"` branches have the same gap.

## Instructions

1. Open `plugins/html-visualization/skills/html-visualize/references/feedback.md`.
2. In Step 4 (~line 181), before "Apply every comment...", add an explicit step: if the content came from a file path, Read that file first — Edit/Write requires a prior Read in the same session.
3. Apply the same Read-before-edit wording to the `action: "apply"` and `action: "submit"` branches (~lines 189-202).

## Files to Modify

- `plugins/html-visualization/skills/html-visualize/references/feedback.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] feedback.md Step 4 instructs Reading the source file before applying comments
- [ ] The apply and submit branches carry the same Read-before-edit instruction
- [ ] Re-rendering an existing document and applying a comment no longer errors on the first Edit/Write

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Fixed. feedback.md Step 4 lead-in plus the action:apply and action:submit branches now instruct Reading the source file before Edit/Write. Complementary to the existing fileless-source case (conversation-only content needs no Read).

title: Fix monitoring-surfaced plugin defects
description:
## Outcome

Three recurring defects surfaced by session-transcript monitoring analysis (169 episodes via `scripts/analyze-sessions.py`) are fixed: html-feedback file-edit failures, beads-plan dependency-type errors, and a monitoring-script aliasing gap that orphans 71 episodes.

## Findings (verified by reviewer)

- **F1** — html-feedback edits a source file without Reading it first; 3/4 sliced episodes hit identical `File has not been read yet` error.
- **F2** — beads-plan/beads-work agents run `bd dep add` across issue types; 7 beads-plan + 1 beads-work episodes failed with `epics can only block other epics`. Rule exists in `beads-issue-workflow.md:64` but `planning.md` never surfaces it.
- **F3+F7** — `analyze-sessions.py` lacks a rename alias for `html-ask`->`html-visualization` (71 episodes orphaned) and has no skill-level alias map (summary rows fragmented across renamed skills).

## Dropped findings

- F4 (`bd comment` flags) — mis-diagnosed; skill text is correct, agents improvised the bad flag.
- F5 (`disable-model-invocation` rejections) — intended harness behavior, not a defect.
- F6 (project-analyze-monitoring-data ordering) — too vague; no transcript evidence pinning the skill text.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Re-rendering an existing document in html-feedback no longer errors on the first Edit/Write
- [ ] `planning.md` states the `bd dep add` same-type constraint at the dependency step
- [ ] Re-running the indexer moves the ~71 `html-ask` episodes into `html-visualization` and clears it from the unmatched table

title: marketplace.json plugin versions stale (all 5 plugins out of sync with plugin.json)
description:
## Description

`.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` versions have not been bumped since v1.1.0 (4 plugins) / v1.2.0 (claude-catppuccin). The per-plugin `plugin.json` files are at v1.4.1. Marketplace consumers installing via marketplace metadata see stale version numbers and stale plugin descriptions.

## Where discovered

During pre-release validation for the next plugin release. `plugin-dev:plugin-validator` flagged this when run against `beads-tasks`.

## Reproduction

```bash
cat .claude-plugin/marketplace.json | jq '.plugins[] | {name, version}'
find plugins -name plugin.json -path "*/.claude-plugin/*" -exec jq -r '"\(.name): \(.version)"' {} \;
```

The two outputs disagree for every plugin.

## Root cause

`docs/RELEASING.md` only documents bumping `plugin.json` version fields. The marketplace.json mirror was never added to the release checklist, so every release since v1.1.0 has silently desynced it.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 5 plugin entries in `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` are bumped to match the current `plugin.json` version
- [ ] `docs/RELEASING.md` step list includes bumping marketplace.json (or a script that does it automatically)
- [ ] After the fix, `jq` comparison of marketplace.json versions vs plugin.json versions returns no mismatches

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Fixed as part of v1.5.0 release: bumped all 5 marketplace.json entries to 1.5.0 to match plugin.json, and updated docs/RELEASING.md to require marketplace.json bumps plus a diff check in step 3 of the release procedure.

title: Acceptance Review: Validate Agents and Skills
description:
## Description
Gate task for the "Validate agents and skills" epic. Verify that all 15 validation tasks are closed, that all discovered defects are properly filed as bugs with complete information, and that the overall validation coverage is complete.

## Instructions
1. Run `bd show claude-dev-tools-sei` to see the epic and its children.
2. Verify all 15 implementation tasks (claude-dev-tools-sei.1 through claude-dev-tools-sei.15) are in `closed` status.
3. Run `bd list --type=bug` to see any bugs filed during validation. For each bug, verify:
   - It has an exact file path
   - It has a specific section/heading reference
   - It has a clear problem description
   - It has a concrete "what it should say instead" recommendation
4. Check that no validation task was closed with "skipped" or without a findings summary in its comments.
5. Confirm the epic's success criteria are met.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 15 validation tasks are closed
- [ ] Every bug filed during validation has: exact file, section, problem description, recommended fix
- [ ] No validation task closed without a findings summary
- [ ] Epic success criteria confirmed met

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
Verification passed. All 3 acceptance criteria met:
1. All 15 validation tasks closed (sei.1–sei.15).
2. All 14 bugs have exact file path, section, problem description, and recommended fix.
3. All tasks now have findings summaries (retroactively added after verifier flagged missing comments).

Recommending closure.

title: Validate all agents and skills: consistency and correctness
description:
## Description
Validate every agent and skill in this repository for two properties:
1. **Consistency** — no internal contradictions, no redundant information duplicated from referenced docs
2. **Correctness** — referenced file paths exist, commands/tools are valid, internal doc links resolve

When a defect is found, the validator files a bug with: exact file path, specific section, description of the problem, and what should be there instead.

## Scope
- 3 agents in `plugins/beads-tasks/agents/`
- 10 skills across all plugins (with complexity-review split into 3 use-case tasks)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 15 agent/skill validation tasks closed
- [ ] Any discovered defects filed as bugs with exact details
- [ ] Acceptance review task complete

title: Migrate project-docs commands to user-invocable skills
description:
## Description
Replace the four commands in `plugins/project-docs/commands/` with four user-invocable, model-non-invocable skills following the beads-tasks pattern (`user-invocable: true`, `disable-model-invocation: true`). The existing `project-docs` skill stays unchanged as the model-only workflow engine.

## Motivation
Commands are a weaker abstraction than skills for this plugin. Skills allow the same `user-invocable` / `disable-model-invocation` split that beads-tasks uses, making the plugin's entry points user-only and keeping all real workflow logic inside the model-only `project-docs` skill.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Four new user-invocable skills created (`create-docs`, `init-or-update-docs`, `improve-doc`, `review-docs`)
- [ ] `plugins/project-docs/commands/` directory deleted
- [ ] `plugins/project-docs/README.md` updated to reflect the skill-based structure
- [ ] Acceptance review task complete

title: task
description:
## Description
The three agent files and the plugin README still reference either the obsolete `coder-beads` skill name or the ambiguous `beads-tasks` skill (which after the refactor is a plugin name containing three skills, not a single SKILL.md). After the refactor, the canonical reference paths are explicit beads-core reference files. This task updates all agent files and the README to reflect reality.

## Instructions

For each agent file:

### 2. Update README.md

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter

All stale beads-tasks skill (ticket-rules) references replaced with explicit beads-core/references/ticket-rules.md paths in tasker.md and verifier.md. reviewer.md had no stale paths. title: task
description:
## Description
Verify the refactor is complete and correct before closing the epic.

title: task
description:
## Description
Replace the current stub in beads-work/SKILL.md with a full implementation of the execution skill. When invoked, Claude checks what is in progress (with triage for stuck tasks), then picks the next ready task, and runs the execution loop using taskers and verifiers.

## Instructions
Replace the entire content of `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-work/SKILL.md`:

### New structure (top to bottom):

1. **Frontmatter** — keep existing: name=beads-work, user-invocable=true, disable-model-invocation=true. Update description to: "Run the beads execution loop — pick up in-progress or ready tasks and drive them to completion."

2. **Step 1: Triage in_progress tasks**
   - Run `bd list --status=in_progress`
   - For each in_progress task: run `bd show <id>` and check last-updated time and latest comment
   - If a task was last updated >24h ago OR has a comment indicating a blocker: surface it to the user and ask whether to resume, skip, or mark blocked — do NOT auto-resume
   - Only proceed automatically with in_progress tasks that were clearly started in the current session
   - If in_progress tasks exist and are healthy: continue those before starting new work
   - If none: proceed to Step 2

3. **Step 2: Pick from ready queue**
   - Run `bd ready` to find unblocked work
   - For each task selected: move to in_progress before spawning a tasker
   - For independent tasks: spawn taskers in parallel (single message, multiple tool calls)
   - After each batch: apply tracker updates serially, then re-check `bd ready`
   - For acceptance review tasks: spawn verifier, not tasker
   - "Follow beads-core/references/execution-orchestration.md for the full loop."

4. **Step 3: Closure**
   - After all implementation tasks close and acceptance review passes: close the epic
   - "See beads-core/references/execution-orchestration.md for closure order."

5. **Operational rules reference**
   - "Load beads-core/references/core-rules.md for serialized writes, agent delegation, and git safety rules."
   - Note: After resolving deduplication (task taj), keep only one reference per concern. If core-rules.md owns serialized writes, do not also point at execution-orchestration.md for the same rule.

### What NOT to include:
- Planning logic (that is beads-plan's job)
- Issue creation (that is beads-plan's job)
- Discussion or exploration handling

## Files to Modify
- `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-work/SKILL.md` (full implementation replacing stub)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] File triages in_progress tasks before resuming (bd show, age check, blocker check)
- [ ] File describes user-confirmation before auto-resuming stale in_progress tasks (>24h or has blocker comment)
- [ ] File checks bd ready when no healthy in_progress tasks exist
- [ ] File says to move task to in_progress before spawning tasker
- [ ] File references execution-orchestration.md for the full loop
- [ ] File references core-rules.md for shared operational rules
- [ ] No planning or issue-creation content
- [ ] File is under 70 lines

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented — 48-line SKILL.md replacing stub. In_progress triage (bd show, age check, blocker check), user confirmation before auto-resuming stale tasks, bd ready fallback, move-before-spawn rule, references execution-orchestration.md and core-rules.md. All 8 acceptance criteria pass.

title: task
description:
## Description
Complete rewrite of beads-plan/SKILL.md plus an update to planning.md. The new SKILL.md opens with the intake decision, stays planning-only, and delegates all workflow steps to beads-core references. The actual step-by-step planning workflow moves into planning.md as a named section.

## Instructions

### Part A — Update beads-core/references/planning.md
Add a clearly named **"Planning Workflow"** section to planning.md containing the step-by-step process (currently in beads-plan/SKILL.md lines 142-149):
1. Review the current conversation to understand what to build or fix
2. Confirm context is sufficient (via planning-intake.md — this is done before arriving here)
3. Choose epic+tasks or standalone tasks based on complexity (use Decision Framework from core-rules.md)
4. Create the epic and tasks — follow the issue structure rules in this file and beads-issue-workflow.md
5. Set dependencies with `bd dep add`
6. Present the plan to the user — brief summary of what will be built and in what order

Place this section near the top of planning.md, before the existing content on issue types and quality bar.

### Part B — Rewrite plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-plan/SKILL.md
Replace the entire content with this structure:

1. **Frontmatter**: name=beads-plan, user-invocable=true, disable-model-invocation=true. Description: "Create beads issues for the current conversation — determines what to plan, asks questions if context is missing, then creates epics, tasks, and acceptance-review tasks."

2. **Step 1 — Intake**: Load and follow `beads-core/references/planning-intake.md`. Either proceed (context is sufficient) or ask questions until it is. Do not proceed to Step 2 until there is enough to write a ticket.

3. **Step 2 — Plan**: Follow the **Planning Workflow** section of `beads-core/references/planning.md` to create the epics, tasks, and acceptance-review task. Load `beads-core/references/beads-issue-workflow.md` for label and dependency rules.

4. **Step 3 — Rules**: "For shared operational rules (serialized writes, agent delegation, git safety), see `beads-core/references/core-rules.md`."

### What NOT to include in the new SKILL.md:
- Any execution trigger content (bd ready, spawn taskers, in_progress)
- Any duplicated core rules — reference core-rules.md instead
- Any planning workflow steps — those are now in planning.md
- The four-use-case section

## Files to Modify
- `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/references/planning.md` (add Planning Workflow section)
- `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-plan/SKILL.md` (full rewrite)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] planning.md has a named "Planning Workflow" section with the 6 steps above
- [ ] beads-plan/SKILL.md opens with Step 1 (intake, references planning-intake.md)
- [ ] beads-plan/SKILL.md Step 2 says "follow the Planning Workflow section of planning.md"
- [ ] No execution trigger content in beads-plan/SKILL.md
- [ ] No duplicated core rules in beads-plan/SKILL.md
- [ ] beads-plan/SKILL.md is under 60 lines

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
Reviewer finding (Finding 6): the task instructs the new beads-plan/SKILL.md to 'Load planning.md and beads-issue-workflow.md and follow their instructions' but those reference files contain bash examples, not a step-by-step workflow tailored to the current conversation. If the skill is reduced to 'load X and Y, follow them', the workflow disappears — the 60-line target is achievable only by deleting workflow steps.

Two options: (a) keep the explicit planning process steps (current beads-plan/SKILL.md lines 142-149) inside the new SKILL.md and have those steps cite the reference files for detail; (b) move the planning workflow section into planning.md itself, and have SKILL.md say 'Step 2: follow the Planning Workflow section of beads-core/references/planning.md'.

**Awaiting user decision before implementing.**

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Decision recorded (Option B): Move the step-by-step planning workflow into beads-core/references/planning.md as a named 'Planning Workflow' section. beads-plan/SKILL.md will say 'follow the Planning Workflow section in beads-core/references/planning.md' — no workflow steps duplicated in the SKILL.md itself. Task scope includes updating planning.md to contain that workflow section (extracting from current beads-plan/SKILL.md lines 142-149).

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented. planning.md has 'Planning Workflow' section (6 steps) near top; old 'Planning workflow' section renamed to 'Issue structure checklist' to avoid ambiguity. SKILL.md rewritten to 22 lines — 3 steps, no duplicated rules or execution content. All acceptance criteria pass.

title: task
description:
## Description
Create a dedicated reference file for the decision logic that beads-plan executes at startup: scan the conversation, decide whether there is enough context to plan, and either proceed or ask targeted questions. Invoking /beads-tasks:beads-plan IS the declaration of planning intent — no discussion/exploration branch is needed.

## Instructions
Create `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/references/planning-intake.md` with the following structure:

### Section 1: Decision algorithm
Describe how to assess whether the current conversation has enough context to create beads issues:

**Sufficient context signals** (any of these → proceed to planning):
- A feature, improvement, or change the user wants to build — described with enough specificity to write a ticket
- A bug or problem they want fixed — with observable symptoms or reproduction steps
- An explicit scope ("rewrite X", "add Y to Z", "fix the issue where...")
- Answers to what-to-build AND what-done-looks-like already present in the conversation

**Insufficient context** (ask questions when these are missing):
- What to build or fix is vague or absent
- The desired outcome is unclear
- No way to write testable acceptance criteria yet

### Section 2: Intake questions (ask only what is missing)
When context is insufficient, ask only the questions whose answers are not already in the conversation:

1. What do you want to build or fix? (scope — required)
2. What is the desired outcome, or what problem does this solve? (required)
3. Are there known constraints, files, or systems involved? (optional — ask if scope is ambiguous)
4. How would you verify it works — what does "done" look like?

For question 4: reference the planner quality bar in `beads-core/references/planning.md` — the answer should satisfy the criteria listed there (testable acceptance criteria, no unresolved questions). Do NOT restate those criteria here.

### Section 3: When to stop asking and plan
Stop asking as soon as questions 1 and 2 are answered with enough specificity that a ticket description can be written. At that point, proceed immediately — do not ask further questions.

## Files to Modify
- `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/references/planning-intake.md` (create)
- `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/SKILL.md` (add routing entry: "Planning intake decision logic → references/planning-intake.md")

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] planning-intake.md exists with all three sections
- [ ] Decision algorithm lists concrete "sufficient" and "insufficient" signals
- [ ] Intake questions are ordered and scoped to what is missing (not a fixed list always asked)
- [ ] Question 4 references planning.md quality bar rather than restating it
- [ ] "When to stop asking" rule is stated explicitly
- [ ] No discussion/exploration branch — skill invocation implies planning intent
- [ ] beads-core/SKILL.md routing table has entry for planning-intake.md

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
Reviewer finding (Finding 5): planning-intake.md has no exit path for discussion/exploration. When a user invokes beads-plan to explore architecture or ask tradeoffs — without intent to create issues — the skill will still push them through intake questions.

Two options: (a) add a third branch to planning-intake.md: 'if user signals exploration intent, do not push planning — but still apply the beads-must-reflect-reality rule from core-rules.md if decisions emerge'; (b) accept that beads-plan is strictly planning-only and move discussion handling + beads-must-reflect-reality into core-rules.md so beads-work and agents inherit it.

Reviewer also flagged (Finding 10): the intake questions in Section 2 (what to build, constraints, done criteria) duplicate the planner quality bar in planning.md lines 13-24. One canonical location should own this checklist — either point Section 2 at planning.md's quality bar, or move it here and cross-reference from planning.md.

**Awaiting user decision on both before finalizing this task.**

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Decision recorded: /beads-tasks:beads-plan invocation IS the planning intent — no discussion/exploration branch needed. planning-intake.md has exactly two paths: (1) enough context in the conversation → proceed to planning, (2) not enough context → ask targeted questions until it is. The intake questions should reference planning.md's quality bar rather than restate it (resolves Finding 10 duplication concern).

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: planning-intake.md created with all three sections. Decision algorithm covers sufficient/insufficient signals. Intake questions reference planning.md quality bar for Q4. Stop-asking rule explicit. No exploration branch. SKILL.md routing table updated.

title: task
description:
## Description
Extract all shared operational rules from beads-plan/SKILL.md into a new reference file so that beads-plan, beads-work, and agents can all reference it without duplication. Also fix beads-core/SKILL.md's description (currently misleading) and eliminate the duplication between the new core-rules.md and execution-orchestration.md.

## Instructions
1. Create `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/references/core-rules.md`
2. Extract and consolidate these sections from the current `beads-plan/SKILL.md`:
   - Serialized writes rule (all beads writes are orchestrator-only)
   - Issue-before-execution rule
   - Priority is numeric (0-4)
   - Beads must reflect reality
   - Agent delegation table (tasker / reviewer / verifier — when to spawn, what they do)
   - Parallel execution and after-agents protocol
   - Git safety rules (never force push, skip hooks, amend pushed commits, commit secrets)
   - Beads core philosophy (closed work not reopened, acceptance review blocks not approves, history immutable)
3. Check `execution-orchestration.md` for overlapping content (serialized writes rule, parallelization rule, follow-up bug pattern). For each overlap: move the canonical paragraph to core-rules.md and replace it in execution-orchestration.md with a one-line cross-reference ("See core-rules.md — serialized writes rule"). Do NOT restate the rule in both files.
4. Update `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/SKILL.md`:
   - Rewrite the frontmatter `description` field to: "Shared reference library for beads workflows — loaded by beads-plan, beads-work, and beads agents; not invoked directly"
   - Confirm `user-invocable: false` remains correct
   - Add routing entries for core-rules.md: "Shared operational rules, git safety, agent delegation → references/core-rules.md"
5. Do NOT delete anything from beads-plan/SKILL.md yet — that happens in the rewrite task (which depends on this one)

## Files to Modify
- `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/references/core-rules.md` (create)
- `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/references/execution-orchestration.md` (remove duplicated paragraphs, add cross-references)
- `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/SKILL.md` (rewrite description, add routing entry)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] core-rules.md exists and contains all 8 rule groups listed above
- [ ] Each rule appears in exactly one file — no rule is stated in both core-rules.md and execution-orchestration.md
- [ ] execution-orchestration.md cross-references core-rules.md wherever content was removed
- [ ] beads-core/SKILL.md description reflects "shared references only, not invoked directly"
- [ ] beads-core/SKILL.md routing table has an entry for core-rules.md
- [ ] No changes made to beads-plan/SKILL.md or beads-work/SKILL.md

title: epic
description:
## Description
The beads-tasks plugin needs a clean separation of concerns and elimination of duplication:

1. **beads-plan is overloaded** — it handles planning, execution triggering, ad-hoc work, AND discussion. It should do one thing: take the current conversation and turn it into beads issues. The decision logic (does this conversation have planning context?) is buried at the bottom rather than up front.

2. **beads-work is a stub** — it exists as a placeholder with no content. It should own the execution loop: check in_progress tasks, or pick the next ready task, and run it.

3. **Shared rules are duplicated** — core rules (serialized writes, priority scheme, git safety, agent delegation, beads philosophy) live in beads-plan/SKILL.md and are not shared with beads-work or agents.

## Files Involved
- `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-plan/SKILL.md` (rewrite)
- `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-work/SKILL.md` (implement from stub)
- `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/SKILL.md` (update routing)
- `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/references/core-rules.md` (new)
- `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/references/planning-intake.md` (new)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] beads-plan/SKILL.md opens with decision logic: does conversation have planning context? If yes, plan. If no, ask targeted questions.
- [ ] beads-plan/SKILL.md is scoped to planning only — no execution trigger, no ad-hoc work handling
- [ ] beads-work/SKILL.md implements the full execution loop (in_progress first, then bd ready, then taskers)
- [ ] All shared operational rules live in beads-core/references/core-rules.md
- [ ] beads-core/SKILL.md routing table covers all reference files including the two new ones
- [ ] Acceptance review confirms no duplication and correct behavior per skill
close_reason: All implementation tasks closed. Acceptance review passed. Refactor complete.

title: Research: evaluate new beads features and decide what to adopt in beads-tasks plugin
description:
## Description
A gap analysis identified 7 beads features that are available but not used in the plugin. This research task must evaluate each feature in the context of the plugin's AI-agent orchestration workflow and produce a concrete adopt/skip decision for each, with implementation tasks for anything worth adopting.

## Context: Features to evaluate

1. **`bd prime --hook-json`** — Replace the static `prime.md` SessionStart hook with a dynamic one. `bd prime` injects project memories, current in-progress state, and sync status. `bd remember` for cross-session project facts. Would eliminate the maintenance burden of keeping `prime.md` current.

2. **`bd update --claim`** — Atomic in_progress assignment (sets assignee + status in one write). Prevents race conditions in parallel-agent setups. Currently the orchestrator does a two-step non-atomic write.

3. **`gh:pr` and `gh:run` gate types** — Model PR-merge and CI-green waits as native gates instead of optimistically closing beads issues when a PR is only opened. Would prevent starting dependent work before code actually lands.

4. **`decision` issue type** — First-class searchable issue type for architectural and design decisions. Currently the plugin buries decisions in `bd comments add`. A `decision` issue is cross-linkable and survives context compaction.

5. **`bd search` before filing bugs** — Add a duplicate-prevention step to the tasker and orchestrator before creating any new bug. Missing today.

6. **Execution metadata on tickets** — Planner can embed `execution_suggested_model`, `execution_agent_type`, etc. in ticket metadata at creation time. Would let tickets self-describe routing instead of hardcoding it in agent files.

7. **`bd stale` at session start** — Surface abandoned in-progress issues from sessions where agents lost context. Missing from the session-open checklist.

## Instructions
1. For each feature: evaluate whether it genuinely improves the plugin's AI-agent workflow or adds unnecessary complexity.
2. For features worth adopting: define exactly how they would change the plugin (which files, which instructions, what new behavior).
3. For features to skip: give a one-line reason.
4. For each "adopt" decision: create a concrete, executable beads implementation task with instructions, file targets, and testable acceptance criteria.
5. Consider whether any features interact or should be adopted together.
6. Do NOT implement any changes — output only the implementation tasks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Adopt/skip decision documented for each of the 7 features
- [ ] Each "adopt" decision has a concrete implementation task
- [ ] No implementation tasks require guessing — all have file targets and specific instructions
- [ ] Features with interactions are noted and sequenced correctly
close_reason: Superseded by claude-dev-tools-hru. The concrete refactor plan (separate beads-plan/beads-work, consolidate into beads-core) makes this research task unnecessary — decisions are already encoded in the implementation tasks.

title: Research: resolve the 5 conflicts in beads-tasks plugin and design single-source-of-truth structure
description:
## Description
A review identified 5 concrete conflicts across `prime.md`, `coder-beads/SKILL.md`, and the three agent files. This research task must analyze each conflict, decide the authoritative location and formulation, and output a set of concrete implementation tasks to execute the cleanup.

## Context: The 5 conflicts

1. **`coder-beads` skill is written for orchestrators but subagents are told to load it** — `tasker.md`, `reviewer.md`, `verifier.md` all instruct agents to load `coder-beads`, but the skill's content (`SKILL.md`) is authored exclusively from the orchestrator's perspective. A tasker loading it receives conflicting role instructions.

2. **Verifier's delegation entry differs between files** — `prime.md` scopes the verifier to any "verification needed" situation and says it returns "recommended tracker actions"; `SKILL.md` is narrower and omits tracker-action output from the verifier's return contract.

3. **No-gate-type constraint only in `prime.md`** — The "do NOT use native beads `gate` type" rule is stated twice in `prime.md` but absent from `verifier.md` (which owns acceptance review execution) and from `SKILL.md`.

4. **`/plan` mode instruction conflict** — `SKILL.md` mandates entering `/plan` mode for any discussion or planning. `prime.md` describes a "Discussion / Exploration" use case that explicitly does NOT push into planning structure prematurely, and never mentions `/plan`.

5. **Verifier can/cannot close issues directly** — The `verifier.md` closure table says "YES — Close with evidence" but the "What You Do NOT Do" section and all scope descriptions say the verifier returns "recommended close reason to the caller" and must NOT modify tracker state directly.

Additionally, `prime.md` and `SKILL.md` share near-verbatim content for: beads-is-the-tracker rule, serialized writes rule, issue-before-execution, priority-is-numeric, beads-must-reflect-reality, subagent delegation table, parallel execution + after-agents protocol, and session close protocol.

## Instructions
1. For each of the 5 conflicts: decide the single authoritative location and the correct formulation.
2. For the prime.md / SKILL.md duplication: decide what belongs in each file and what should be referenced rather than repeated.
3. For the subagent files: identify which overlapping content (tracker comment discipline, bug discovery, ticket readiness checks) can be consolidated and where.
4. For each decision: create a concrete, executable beads implementation task with instructions, file targets, and testable acceptance criteria.
5. Do NOT implement any changes — output only the implementation tasks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each of the 5 conflicts has a documented resolution decision
- [ ] The prime.md / SKILL.md duplication boundary is clearly defined
- [ ] Each decision maps to one or more concrete beads implementation tasks
- [ ] No implementation tasks require guessing — all have file targets and specific instructions
close_reason: Superseded by claude-dev-tools-hru. The 5 conflicts are addressed directly by the refactor tasks rather than through a separate research phase.

title: beads-tasks plugin: cleanup contradictions and adopt new beads features
description:
## Description
The beads-tasks plugin has grown organically and now has two problems that need to be addressed:

1. **Internal contradictions and duplication** — `prime.md` and `coder-beads/SKILL.md` are near-verbatim copies of each other, the five conflicts identified in the review need to be resolved, and subagent files have overlapping content that should live in one place.

2. **Missing beads features** — Several beads features that would improve the plugin's correctness and expressiveness are not used: `bd prime --hook-json`, `bd update --claim`, gate issue types, `decision` issue type, `bd search` for dedup, and others.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 5 identified conflicts resolved with a single source of truth for each rule
- [ ] Duplication between prime.md and SKILL.md eliminated
- [ ] New beads features evaluated and adopted where appropriate
- [ ] Acceptance review task complete
close_reason: Superseded by claude-dev-tools-hru which covers the same goals with a concrete implementation plan.

title: analyze-sessions.py: fix plugin and skill rename aliases
description:
## Description

`scripts/analyze-sessions.py` mis-buckets renamed plugins and skills:

- **Plugin layer (data loss):** `html-ask` was renamed to `html-visualization` (commit adb6901). `RENAME_ALIASES` only maps `complexity-review`, so 71 episodes with `attributionPlugin: html-ask` fall into the unmatched bucket and out of `dataset.json`. All 71 records predate the rename — confirmed historical data.
- **Skill layer (cosmetic):** the per-skill summary table keys on the raw `attribution_skill` string (~line 604) with no skill-level alias map. Renamed skills fragment into separate rows (e.g. `coder-docs`/`project-docs`, `executes-tests`/`project-executes-tests`, `visualize-html`). The plugin still resolves, so no episodes are lost — only the table splits.

## Instructions

1. Add `"html-ask": "html-visualization"` to `RENAME_ALIASES` (~line 47).
2. Add a parallel `SKILL_RENAME_ALIASES` map and normalize the per-skill aggregation key (~line 604) through it before grouping.
3. Update the `RENAME_ALIASES` snippet in `docs/MONITORING.md` (~line 63) to match.

## Files to Modify

- `scripts/analyze-sessions.py`
- `docs/MONITORING.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `RENAME_ALIASES` includes `html-ask` -> `html-visualization`
- [ ] A `SKILL_RENAME_ALIASES` map exists and is applied before per-skill aggregation
- [ ] Re-running the indexer removes `html-ask` from the unmatched table and raises the `html-visualization` episode count by ~71
- [ ] The summary table no longer shows old and new names of the same skill as separate rows
- [ ] `docs/MONITORING.md` reflects the updated alias map

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Fixed. RENAME_ALIASES gets html-ask->html-visualization; new SKILL_RENAME_ALIASES map applied at per-skill aggregation; MONITORING.md updated. Runtime: 181->187 episodes, html-ask cleared from unmatched table, 23->16 skill rows (fragmented pairs merged). Fixture passes.

Produce a verification report and a close recommendation.

title: Add the footer (message textarea, Send, Save) to the visualize template
description:
Add an always-present footer to `visualize-template.html`: one freeform
"message to Claude" textarea, a Send button, and a Save button. Keep it minimal
and themed with the existing `--vis-*` tokens.

- **Send**: CSRF POST to `/submit`, reusing the server's injected token, per the
  new round-trip in bmh.1. Empty message + Send acts as a plain close.
- **Save**: client-side download of the rendered HTML page. The saved file MUST
  be clean — strip the server-injected `<script>const CSRF_TOKEN=...</script>`
  and neutralize the Send button (it cannot reach a dead server when opened
  offline) so the saved copy is genuinely self-contained.
- **JS must be inline.** Do NOT add `assets/visualize/app.js` or any `/assets/…`
  reference — `visualize.md` requires the page to open correctly as a `file://`
  URL. Inline only.

No ask-mode widget machinery — one textarea plus plain buttons.

Note: the Save half is pure client-side and does not actually depend on bmh.1;
it may be implemented and landed independently if convenient.

Send: CSRF POST /submit with freeform; verified end-to-end — non-empty writes feedback file + exit 0, empty exits 0 with no file. Save: clones DOM, strips every script containing CSRF_TOKEN (injected token + footer script), disables Send → clean self-contained file. No /assets/ added. server.js needed no change (token already injected unconditionally). 59 tests pass.

title: Extend the --no-wait server with a submit/close round-trip
description:
## Step 0 — Verification spike (do this first)

Before touching `bin/server.js`, confirm the assumption the message feature
rests on: does the harness re-invoke Claude — and does Claude then read the
feedback file — when a background `--no-wait` server exits *after* Claude has
already continued past the serve step? See the epic's Open Questions.

- If reliable → proceed with the round-trip below.
- If not → the message feature degrades to "saved, read when Claude is next
  active." Update the epic and docs to describe that instead of promising a
  live round-trip; do not build a round-trip that silently drops messages.

## Server change

Visualize mode currently serves with `--no-wait` and rejects POST `/submit`
(405). Change the `--no-wait` server to accept a one-shot round-trip:

- Non-empty message submitted → write a feedback file, exit 0 (harness
  notifies / re-invokes Claude; Claude reads the feedback file).
- Empty message / plain close → exit 0 silently, write NO feedback file
  (Claude is not re-invoked).
- Timeout (default 1800s) → exit 0 silently — same outcome as an empty close.

The server keeps running as a background process; Claude does not block on it.

Payload shape: reuse the field name `freeform` (matching ask mode) for the
message text; keep the payload minimal. Coordinate with bmh.3 so the shape is
documented once.

Update `bin/server.js` and the `--no-wait` server tests to cover all three
paths: silent empty-close, silent timeout, and non-empty re-invoke.
notes: Exit-code semantics (observed during testing): the blocking ask/feedback servers exit NON-ZERO (code 2) on a no-submission timeout, which the harness reports as a 'failed' task. The --no-wait server must NOT inherit that. For visualize mode 'no message' is the normal expected outcome, so on both timeout and empty-close the --no-wait server must exit 0 (silent, not 'failed'). Only a genuine server error should be non-zero. Add a test asserting --no-wait timeout exits 0.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done. --no-wait server now accepts POST /submit: non-empty freeform writes a feedback file + exit 0; empty/close exits 0 silently with no file; timeout exits 0 silently (not the blocking servers' exit-2). 405 branch removed. test-server.sh: 3 paths covered, 35 tests pass. Step 0 spike resolved positive by orchestrator (harness re-invokes on background-server exit — observed in-session).

title: Add a feedback footer (message + Save) to html-visualize visualize mode
description:
## Outcome

Every `visualize`-mode page (display-only mode of the html-visualize skill) gets
a standard footer: a freeform "message to Claude" textarea, a **Send** button,
and a **Save** button. This gives the user a clean way to close the server and
to react to what they see, and a way to keep the page.

## Background

Found while testing the skill. Visualize mode's `--no-wait` server has no clean
shutdown — it dies only on the 30-min timeout or a manual `kill` — and there is
no channel for the user to respond to the visualization. The user also often
wants to keep the rendered page.

## Behavior (decided)

- The footer is **always on** for visualize mode. No opt-out flag for now — add
  one later only if a concrete need appears.
- Visualize mode stays **non-blocking**: Claude serves the page and continues
  immediately; the server runs as a background process.
- **Send** with a non-empty message → server writes a feedback file, exits 0,
  the harness re-invokes Claude with the message (asynchronously, whenever the
  user submits). See Open Questions — this is the load-bearing assumption.
- **Send/close with an empty message** → silent server shutdown, no feedback
  file, Claude is NOT re-invoked. Never wake Claude for an empty submit.
- **Timeout** (default 1800s, user never sends) → silent shutdown, no feedback
  file — same outcome as an empty close.
- **Save** → lets the user download the rendered HTML page locally. Client-side;
  works regardless of server state. The saved copy must be clean (no injected
  CSRF token, Send neutralized).

## Constraints

- Minimal — one textarea plus plain buttons. No ask-mode widget machinery. If
  structured questions are needed, that is ask mode.
- The footer JS must be **inline** — visualize pages must still open correctly
  as a `file://` URL, so no `/assets/…` reference may be added.
- `file://` caveat: a saved copy opened offline cannot reach the server, so its
  Send button is inert. Document this; the Save button is the intended path for
  "I want to keep this".

## Open Questions

- **Does the deferred re-invocation actually work?** The message feature assumes
  the harness re-invokes Claude — and Claude then reads the feedback file — when
  a background `--no-wait` server exits *after* Claude has already continued. In
  ask mode Claude is blocking and explicitly waiting; in visualize mode it has
  moved on or the conversation may have ended. bmh.1 starts with a verification
  spike. If deferred re-invocation is unreliable, the message feature degrades
  to "saved, read when Claude is next active" and the behavior above + the docs
  must be revised to say so rather than promise a live round-trip.

## Success criteria

- Every visualize-mode page renders the footer.
- The `--no-wait` server supports the submit/close round-trip per the rules
  above; tests cover empty-close (silent), timeout (silent), and non-empty-send
  (re-invoke).
- The Save button produces a clean, self-contained HTML file.
- `visualize.md`, `serve.md`, `SKILL.md`, `README.md`, `html-visualize-demo`'s
  `SKILL.md`, and the visualize template reflect the new behavior, including the
  `file://` caveat, the payload shape, and the always-on / no-opt-out decision.

title: Introduce a shared --hv-* design-token layer across all modes
description:
## Description
ask, feedback, and visualize each define their own colours/spacing. Introduce one shared token layer so all modes are visually consistent and light/dark behaves identically.

## Instructions
1. Audit `plugins/html-visualization/assets/ask/style.css`, `assets/feedback/style.css`, and the `<style>` block in `skills/html-visualize/references/visualize-template.html`; list every colour/spacing value and the divergences.
2. Create `plugins/html-visualization/assets/shared/tokens.css` defining `--hv-bg`, `--hv-text`, `--hv-surface`, `--hv-border`, `--hv-accent`, `--hv-muted`, a type scale and a spacing scale, with `prefers-color-scheme: dark` overrides.
3. `assets/ask/style.css` and `assets/feedback/style.css` — link/`@import` `tokens.css` (server serves `/assets/`, so `/assets/shared/tokens.css` resolves) and replace duplicated colour literals with `var(--hv-*)`.
4. `references/visualize-template.html` — replace the `--vis-*` block with the `--hv-*` token block inlined (visualize must stay self-contained for `file://`); add a sync comment in `tokens.css` and the template noting the two copies must match.
5. `references/visualize.md` — update all `--vis-*` references to `--hv-*`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `assets/ask/style.css` and `assets/feedback/style.css` reference `tokens.css`; no duplicated colour literals remain

- [ ] `bash tests/run-all.sh` passes

title: Extend feedback mode to cover plan review and brainstorming
description:
## Description
feedback mode is currently scoped to pre-existing user prose. Extend it so it also serves reviewing and brainstorming on Claude-authored plans, which often have no backing file.

## Instructions
1. `plugins/html-visualization/skills/html-visualize-feedback/SKILL.md` — broaden the `description` and body wording to include "a plan or set of ideas you authored"; keep `argument-hint` accurate.
2. `plugins/html-visualization/skills/html-visualize/references/feedback.md`:
   - "Scope of feedback mode" — state content may be Claude-authored (a plan, proposal, brainstormed options), not only a pre-existing document.
   - Step 1 "Decide what to render" — each plan section / idea / option becomes one commentable block.
   - Step 4 Apply step — add the fileless case: when the plan exists only in the conversation, the plan itself is the underlying source; apply comments in context and regenerate.
   - Note `freeform` and "what is missing" comments are the brainstorming "add new ideas" channel, and an Apply round may introduce new options.
3. `plugins/html-visualization/skills/html-visualize/SKILL.md` — update the feedback row of the Mode routing table if its one-line description no longer fits.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `feedback.md` Scope and Step 1 explicitly name plan/brainstorm and Claude-authored content
- [ ] Step 4 documents the fileless-source case for the Apply loop
- [ ] `html-visualize-feedback/SKILL.md` description mentions plans; `argument-hint` still accurate
- [ ] `grep -rn "plan" plugins/html-visualization/skills/html-visualize/references/feedback.md` shows the new guidance
- [ ] No other mode reference doc (ask.md, visualize.md) changed

## Verification
Self-verifiable with no server or browser: run the grep check above and re-read the three edited files.

title: Author the display-only visualize mode + plain-canvas template
description:
## Description
Add the new third mode: display-only `visualize`. When the intent is "show / explain / visualize this" rather than ask questions or annotate prose, the skill builds a chart/diagram/dashboard/interactive demo, serves it via the non-blocking server mode, and lets Claude continue without waiting for a submit. Provide a plain-canvas template asset and authoring guidance for good self-contained visualizations.

## Instructions
1. Create `references/visualize.md` — guidance for rendering a good display-only page: self-contained HTML, when to use SVG / chart libraries / tables, layout, responsiveness; instruct use of the non-blocking server mode from `serve.md`.
2. Create a plain-canvas template asset (e.g. `references/canvas-template.html`) — a minimal self-contained starting point with no submit/widget machinery.
3. Wire the dispatcher in `SKILL.md` to route visualize-intent to this mode and the non-blocking server flag.
4. Decide and document whether a static `file://` fallback is offered when the page is fully self-contained.

## Files to Modify
- `plugins/html-visualization/skills/visualize-html/references/visualize.md` (new)
- `plugins/html-visualization/skills/visualize-html/references/canvas-template.html` (new)
- `plugins/html-visualization/skills/visualize-html/SKILL.md` (dispatch wiring)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `references/visualize.md` gives concrete guidance for good self-contained visualizations
- [ ] A plain-canvas template asset exists with no submit/widget machinery

title: Build visualize-html skill: dispatcher + shared serve reference + migrate ask/feedback modes
description:
## Description
Create the new `visualize-html` skill under `plugins/html-visualization/skills/`. The SKILL.md is a thin dispatcher: it reads the free-text intent and routes to one of three modes. The skill is both model-auto-triggered and user-invocable — it is a SKILL, NOT a slash command (no `commands/` entry; RULES.md Rule 2 stays intact). The shared serve procedure (Node pre-flight, temp-dir, copy template, start server, surface URL, cleanup) is written ONCE in `references/serve.md`. The `ask` and `feedback` mode-specific content (markup contract, submit schema, what-to-render guidance) is migrated from the existing html-ask / html-feedback skills into `references/ask.md` and `references/feedback.md`.

## Instructions
1. Create `skills/visualize-html/SKILL.md` with a triggering description that fires on model auto-detection (about to present 3+ questions, or about to show content for annotation, or about to show a visualization) and supports explicit user invocation. No `commands/` slash command.
2. The SKILL.md body is a dispatcher: classify the intent into `ask` / `feedback` / `visualize` and load the matching `references/<mode>.md`. Document explicit precedence for ambiguous intents (S2): presence of questions to answer -> ask; existing content to revise/annotate -> feedback; otherwise -> visualize.
3. Create `references/serve.md` — the shared procedure. It MUST cover all THREE server cycles: (a) the blocking submit round-trip used by ask, (b) the non-blocking serve-and-continue path used by visualize, and (c) the feedback Apply loop — re-serve on the same captured port via the `.port` file with a fresh `fb-generation` value driving client auto-reload (B3). State explicitly that the Apply-loop port-persistence + `fb-generation` contract lives in `serve.md`.
4. Migrate html-ask content into `references/ask.md` and html-feedback content into `references/feedback.md`, each pointing at `references/serve.md` for the shared steps. Move/keep the existing markup.md, submit-schema.md, template.html assets as needed.
5. When this task lands, also neuter the old `html-ask` and `html-feedback` skills' `description` frontmatter so they no longer auto-trigger (B4) — this prevents nondeterministic skill selection during the window before the retire task (2cl) removes them entirely. The directories themselves are deleted in 2cl.

## Files to Modify
- `plugins/html-visualization/skills/visualize-html/SKILL.md` (new)
- `plugins/html-visualization/skills/visualize-html/references/serve.md` (new)
- `plugins/html-visualization/skills/visualize-html/references/ask.md` (new)
- `plugins/html-visualization/skills/visualize-html/references/feedback.md` (new)
- `plugins/html-visualization/skills/visualize-html/references/` template/markup/schema assets (migrated)
- `plugins/html-visualization/skills/html-ask/SKILL.md` (neuter description frontmatter)
- `plugins/html-visualization/skills/html-feedback/SKILL.md` (neuter description frontmatter)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `visualize-html` skill exists, is auto-triggerable and user-invocable, and adds no slash command
- [ ] SKILL.md dispatches intent to ask / feedback / visualize modes with documented precedence for ambiguous intents
- [ ] `references/serve.md` documents all three server cycles (blocking, non-blocking, Apply loop) including the `.port` + `fb-generation` contract
- [ ] `/visualize-html` ask and feedback flows reproduce current html-ask / html-feedback behavior end-to-end
- [ ] Old html-ask / html-feedback skills no longer auto-trigger (descriptions neutered); directories still present (removed in 2cl)

Old html-ask/html-feedback SKILL.md descriptions neutered (no longer auto-trigger). All internal reference links resolve. tests/run-all.sh: 3 suites, 0 failures. title: server.js: add non-blocking serve-and-continue mode
description:
## Description
The display-only `visualize` mode has no submit step, so the server must not block waiting for feedback. Add a non-blocking serve mode to `plugins/html-visualization/bin/server.js`: serve the page, print the URL, and let the caller continue immediately. The server self-terminates on timeout. The existing blocking (wait-for-submit) mode used by `ask`/`feedback` must remain unchanged.

## Instructions
1. Add a flag (e.g. `--no-wait` / `--display-only`) to `server.js` that selects non-blocking mode.
2. In non-blocking mode: serve the HTML, print the `URL:` line, do NOT print/expect a feedback file, do NOT exit on submit; exit only on the timeout.
3. Keep the default (no flag) behavior identical to today: serve, wait for first submit, write feedback JSON, exit 0.
4. Non-blocking mode default timeout is 1800s (30 min) — same as blocking mode, for consistency — and remains overridable via `--timeout-sec` (N2).

## Files to Modify
- `plugins/html-visualization/bin/server.js`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Non-blocking mode starts, prints the URL, and does not block on a submit
- [ ] Non-blocking mode self-terminates on timeout (default 1800s)
- [ ] Existing blocking submit mode is unchanged (serve, wait, write feedback JSON, exit 0)
- [ ] `--timeout-sec` overrides the timeout in both modes

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented: --no-wait flag in bin/server.js. Non-blocking mode serves + prints URL, rejects POST /submit with 405, exits 0 on timeout (1800s default, --timeout-sec overridable). Default blocking mode unchanged (exit 2 on timeout). Both modes verified. Design decisions: (1) no-wait timeout exits 0 (expected lifecycle, not error); (2) POST /submit returns 405 in no-wait mode. Both acceptable for the visualize mode.

title: Unify html-visualization into one visualize-html skill
description:
## Description
Replace the `html-ask` and `html-feedback` skills in the `html-visualization` plugin with a single skill, `visualize-html`, that takes a free-text intent and internally dispatches to one of three modes: `ask` (structured questions form), `feedback` (inline annotation of existing content), or display-only `visualize` (render a chart/diagram/demo for the user to look at). One shared serve procedure is reused across all modes.

`visualize-html` is a SKILL, not a slash command. No `commands/` entry is added — this keeps RULES.md Rule 2 ("do not add slash commands to this plugin") intact. The skill is both model-auto-triggered AND user-invocable, because Claude Code skills are invocable by the user by nature.

## Outcome
A single `visualize-html` skill replaces the two existing skills. All three modes work end-to-end. The shared serve/temp-dir/cleanup procedure is written once. The plugin name finally matches what the plugin does.

## Success Criteria
- `visualize-html` works as both an auto-triggered skill and a user-invoked skill — with no slash command added.
- `ask` and `feedback` modes reproduce current html-ask / html-feedback behavior.
- New display-only `visualize` mode renders and serves a page via a non-blocking server mode.
- `html-ask` and `html-feedback` skill directories are removed; plugin docs reference only `visualize-html`.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Design decision (B1, from user review): visualize-html is a SKILL, not a slash command. No commands/ directory entry is added, so RULES.md Rule 2 is NOT violated and does not need amending. Skills are user-invocable by nature, which satisfies the 'triggered by models and users' requirement.

title: Write the html-ask SKILL.md
description:
## Description
Write `plugins/html-ask/skills/html-ask/SKILL.md` — the skill that teaches Claude when and how to produce an interactive HTML feedback doc and run the round-trip. Depends on the server CLI (aa9.2) and the asset kit / markup contract (aa9.3).

## Instructions
1. Frontmatter: `name: html-ask`, a `description` that triggers when Claude is about to present a multi-section plan or a batch of decisions, plus explicit user requests.
2. Trigger bar — when to USE (concrete and countable): a plan with 3+ separately-numbered open questions, OR 3+ approve/reject decision points, OR an explicit user request. When NOT to use (explicit negative criteria): <=2 questions, short/quick answers, fast in-chat iteration. When unsure, bias toward NOT triggering.
3. Pre-flight: before building any doc or surfacing a URL, run `node --version`. If `node` is absent, do NOT build the doc — fall back to asking the questions in chat and tell the user why.
4. Build steps: extract the plan/questions from the conversation; copy `references/template.html`; fill content and emit widgets per `references/markup.md`; write the HTML into a unique per-invocation system-temp subdirectory.
5. Server invocation: run `node ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/bin/server.js <temp-html>` as a background process; surface the printed URL to the user; explain the harness re-invokes Claude when the server exits.
6. Read-back: after re-invocation, read `<basename>.feedback.json`, map `answers` by question ID, incorporate `comments`/`verdict`/`freeform`, and continue the original task.

## How to test (self-verify before closing)
- Validate frontmatter: `name` and `description` present and well-formed.
- Run the `plugin-dev:skill-reviewer` agent on the new SKILL.md — confirm trigger-description quality and no over-broad triggering.
- Dry-run walkthrough: confirm the commands documented (`node --version`, `node ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/bin/server.js ...`) match the actual server CLI delivered by aa9.2.
- NOTE: the end-to-end browser smoke is owned by the Acceptance Review task.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `SKILL.md` exists with valid frontmatter (`name`, `description`)
- [ ] Trigger bar has concrete countable positive criteria AND explicit negative criteria
- [ ] Documents the `node --version` pre-flight before any doc or URL is produced
- [ ] Documents the full flow: extract -> build HTML -> run server -> read feedback back
- [ ] References `template.html` and `markup.md` for HTML authoring
- [ ] `plugin-dev:skill-reviewer` raises no trigger-quality blockers

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented plugins/html-ask/skills/html-ask/SKILL.md. plugin-dev:skill-reviewer: PASS, no trigger-quality blockers (concrete countable trigger bar + explicit negative criteria + bias-to-no hedge). One MAJOR finding fixed: placeholder-string mismatch — template.html <title> aligned to '[Claude: replace with document title]' to match <h1> and SKILL.md Step 2c. CLI/startup-output/feedback-path verified against bin/server.js. tests/run-all.sh green (35/35).

title: Build the HTML asset kit (CSS, JS, template, markup contract)
description:
## Description
Build the shared HTML asset kit for `html-ask`: the styling, interaction JS, the reference template Claude copies, and the markup-vocabulary contract. These four files share one widget vocabulary and must stay consistent — kept in a single task so the vocabulary cannot drift.

## Files to create
- `plugins/html-ask/assets/style.css`
- `plugins/html-ask/assets/app.js`
- `plugins/html-ask/skills/html-ask/references/template.html`
- `plugins/html-ask/skills/html-ask/references/markup.md`

## Instructions
1. Define a widget vocabulary (CSS classes + `data-*` attributes + element shapes) covering: text question, single-choice (radio), multi-choice (checkbox), approve/reject, inline-comment anchor, overall verdict, and side-by-side approaches (columns each with own approve/reject).
2. `style.css` — page chrome + styling for every widget; readable, responsive.
3. `app.js`:
   - Structure the feedback-payload assembly as a PURE FUNCTION (e.g. `buildFeedbackPayload(state)` returning the `/submit` object) so it is unit-testable in Node without a browser. Keep DOM wiring thin around it.
   - Attach inline comments to `annotatable` elements; collect answers/comments/verdict.
   - Read the CSRF token injected by the server and include it on `POST /submit`.
   - Render a "submitted — return to Claude" state on success.
   - Provide a "Copy feedback" fallback button that copies the EXACT `/submit` JSON payload (the same schema — NOT a separate markdown format) to the clipboard, so the user can paste it into Claude if the server is unreachable.
4. `template.html` — the skeleton Claude copies: `<head>` linking `/assets/style.css` + `/assets/app.js`, page chrome, one worked example of every widget.
5. `markup.md` — the contract: every class, `data-*` attribute, and element shape the assets expect, so Claude can author HTML without reading `style.css`/`app.js`.

## Submit contract
The `/submit` payload schema and token mechanism are defined in `plugins/html-ask/skills/html-ask/references/submit-schema.md` (created in aa9.1). That file is authoritative — consume it, do not redefine the schema.

## How to test (self-verify before closing)
- `node --check plugins/html-ask/assets/app.js` — no syntax errors.
- Add a Node script-test in `tests/html-ask/script-tests/` that loads `buildFeedbackPayload` and asserts it produces an object matching the `submit-schema.md` shape for representative widget state.
- Cross-check: every class / `data-*` used in `template.html` is documented in `markup.md`, and vice versa.
- Serve `template.html` with `bin/server.js`, fetch `/` and `/assets/*`, confirm 200s and that the token is present in the served HTML.
- NOTE: full in-browser rendering / click-through is verified in the Acceptance Review (manual browser smoke) — a headless tasker cannot click the UI.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `style.css`, `app.js`, `template.html`, `markup.md` created with one consistent vocabulary
- [ ] `app.js` payload assembly is a pure function with a passing Node script-test
- [ ] `app.js` reads the server token and POSTs the `submit-schema.md` shape to `/submit`
- [ ] "Copy feedback" fallback copies the exact `/submit` JSON payload (no second format)
- [ ] `markup.md` documents the full vocabulary; `template.html` uses only documented vocabulary
- [ ] `node --check` passes on `app.js`

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: All four files created (style.css, app.js, template.html, markup.md) + tests/html-ask/script-tests/test-payload.sh. node --check clean; test-payload.sh 13/13; run-all.sh 35/35; full tests/run-all.sh clean. buildFeedbackPayload is a pure UMD-exported function. Cross-check: every class/data-* in template.html documented in markup.md. Decisions: approaches widget answer keys are <qid>-<approach-id> in answers (page-level verdict separate); 410 shows amber already-submitted state; copy-feedback uses same buildFeedbackPayload (no 2nd format); fixed nested-label HTML violation. Full browser click-through deferred to AR.

title: Build the zero-dependency Node feedback server
description:
## Description
Build `plugins/html-ask/bin/server.js` — a zero-dependency Node server that serves a Claude-authored HTML doc plus shared assets, accepts an authenticated feedback POST, and exits one-shot so the harness re-invokes Claude. Also create its script-tests.

## Instructions
1. Use only Node built-in modules (`node:http`, `node:fs`, `node:path`, `node:crypto`, etc.) — no npm dependencies, no install step.
2. CLI: `node server.js <html-file> [--port N] [--timeout-sec N]`. Default timeout = 1800s.
3. Bind `127.0.0.1`. For the port: bind to port `0` and read the OS-assigned port from the listening socket — do NOT scan-for-free-port-then-bind (it races).
4. At startup generate an unguessable token (`crypto.randomUUID()` / `randomBytes`). Inject it into the served HTML (e.g. a `<meta>` tag or `window.__HTML_ASK_TOKEN__`) so `app.js` can read it.
5. Routes:
   - `GET /` serves `<html-file>` with the token injected.
   - `GET /assets/*` serves files from `plugins/html-ask/assets/`. Resolve the path and reject with 404 anything escaping the `assets/` directory (path traversal).
   - `POST /submit` accepts a JSON body.
6. `POST /submit` must be rejected with 403 unless BOTH: the request carries the correct token, AND `Origin`/`Sec-Fetch-Site` headers indicate a same-origin request.
7. On a valid `POST /submit`: write the JSON body to `<html-file-dir>/<basename>.feedback.json`, respond 200 with a short "submitted — return to Claude" page, then shut down and `exit 0`.
8. On timeout with no submit: log a clear message and `exit` non-zero.
9. The skill writes each invocation's HTML into a unique per-invocation temp subdirectory; the server writes the feedback file alongside it so concurrent invocations cannot collide.
10. On startup print to stdout the URL and the absolute feedback-file path.

## Submit contract
The `/submit` payload schema and token mechanism are defined in `plugins/html-ask/skills/html-ask/references/submit-schema.md` (created in aa9.1). That file is authoritative — consume it, do not redefine the schema here.

## How to test (self-verify before closing)
Create `tests/html-ask/script-tests/` (follow existing `tests/` harness conventions). By starting the real server and issuing HTTP requests, cover:
- `GET /` returns the HTML with the token injected.
- `GET /assets/<file>` serves an asset; `GET /assets/../../<path>` returns 404.
- `POST /submit` with valid token + same-origin headers writes `<basename>.feedback.json` verbatim and the process exits 0.
- `POST /submit` with missing/wrong token returns 403 and the server keeps running.
- Timeout with no submit exits non-zero.
Run `bash tests/run-all.sh` and confirm the new tests pass.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Server uses only Node built-in modules (zero dependencies)
- [ ] Serves HTML at `/` (token injected) and assets at `/assets/*`; rejects path traversal with 404; binds 127.0.0.1 via port 0
- [ ] `POST /submit` requires a valid token AND same-origin headers; otherwise 403
- [ ] Valid submit writes `<basename>.feedback.json` and the process exits 0; timeout exits non-zero
- [ ] Startup prints the URL and feedback-file path
- [ ] `tests/html-ask/script-tests/` exists and passes under `bash tests/run-all.sh`

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Implemented plugins/html-ask/bin/server.js (zero-dep Node) + tests/html-ask/script-tests/{run-all.sh,test-server.sh}. 22 script-tests pass; full tests/run-all.sh clean (html-ask 22, marketplace 8, project-docs 100). Server conforms to submit-schema.md contract (verdict enum, X-CSRF-Token, CSRF_TOKEN const, 410 dup, 400 bad verdict, port 0, path-traversal 404). Note: Origin/Sec-Fetch-Site checked only when present (per submit-schema.md); the mandatory unguessable token is the primary CSRF defense — sound, since a cross-origin page cannot read the token. run-all.sh auto-discovers test-*.sh so aa9.3 can drop its test in with no edit.

Document the JSON schema `{ "verdict": string, "answers": { qID: value }, "comments": [ { "anchor": string, "text": string } ], "freeform": string }`, the CSRF token field/header, and field semantics. JSON parse-checks pass; tests/run-all.sh passes (8+68). plugin-dev:plugin-validator: PASS (only a pre-existing repo-wide author-email gmail/dynatrace inconsistency — filed as follow-up). submit-schema.md locks contract decisions for aa9.2/aa9.3: verdict enum approve|approve-with-changes|reject, header X-CSRF-Token, JS const CSRF_TOKEN, 410 on duplicate submit, qID = printable-ASCII no-whitespace, anchor = CSS selector.

title: Build the html-ask plugin
description:
## Description
Build `html-ask`, a skill-only Claude Code plugin in this marketplace. When Claude is about to present a multi-decision plan or a batch of questions, the skill converts that content into an interactive HTML document, serves it via a bundled zero-dependency Node server, the user answers/comments in-browser and submits, and a one-shot server writes the feedback and exits — re-invoking Claude to continue.

## Outcome / Success Criteria
- `plugins/html-ask/` is a valid, installable plugin listed in `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json`
- A bundled zero-dependency Node server serves a Claude-authored HTML doc plus shared assets and accepts an authenticated feedback POST
- The skill triggers appropriately and drives the full round-trip
- End-to-end: Claude builds a doc, the user submits feedback in-browser, Claude reads it back and continues

## Locked design decisions (from planning conversation + review)
- Skill-only — no slash command
- Node server, zero-dependency (Node built-in modules only; no npm install)
- Claude writes the full HTML against a documented markup vocabulary; CSS/JS are shared, server-hosted assets
- HTML + feedback files are written to a unique per-invocation subdirectory of the system temp dir (ephemeral)
- One-shot server lifecycle: serves until first successful submit, writes `*.feedback.json`, then `exit 0`. VERIFIED this session — see epic comment; the harness re-invokes Claude on background-process exit.
- Server binds `127.0.0.1` and picks a free port. The localhost bind is NOT a CSRF boundary — any local browser tab can POST to it. Real protection: an unguessable startup token embedded in the served HTML and checked on `/submit`, plus Origin/Sec-Fetch-Site validation.
- Single source of truth for the `/submit` payload schema and token mechanism: `skills/html-ask/references/submit-schema.md`
- POST `/submit` payload schema: `{verdict, answers, comments, freeform}`

## Verification approach
- Server + `/submit` contract: fully automated script-tests under `tests/html-ask/` (run by `tests/run-all.sh`).
- `app.js` payload assembly: pure function with a Node script-test.
- Full in-browser rendering + click-through: manual browser smoke in the Acceptance Review task (a headless agent cannot click the UI).

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Re-invoke mechanism VERIFIED this session (2026-05-22): ran a backgrounded 'sleep 4; exit 0'; on process exit the harness re-invoked the agent with a task-notification carrying the output-file path. The one-shot-server -> exit 0 -> read feedback file design is sound. Reviewer finding B1 (unverified assumption) is resolved — no spike task needed.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: PLAN-REVIEW GATE SATISFIED. Epic reviewed by beads-tasks:reviewer this session. Findings resolved into tickets: B1 (re-invoke unverified) -> verified empirically, see prior comment; B2 (CSRF) -> token + Origin/Sec-Fetch-Site checks added to aa9.2; B3 (AR boilerplate) -> epic-specific ACs added to a0o; S4 (test conflation) -> aa9.1/aa9.2 reworded + script-tests; S5 (schema duplication) -> single-source submit-schema.md in aa9.1; S6 (RULES.md) -> added to aa9.1; S7 (path traversal) -> aa9.2; S8 (port race) -> bind port 0 in aa9.2; S9 (node preflight) -> aa9.4; S10 (skill over-trigger) -> skill-reviewer gate in a0o; N11 (copy fallback) -> kept, same JSON format; N12 (trigger threshold) -> concrete countable criteria in aa9.4; N13 (basename collision) -> per-invocation temp subdir. No unresolved blocking questions. Proceeding to execution.

title: Write docs/MONITORING.md
description:
## Description

Canonical project doc describing the session-analysis monitoring workflow. Authored to project-docs guidelines: action-first, repo-local anchors, file pointers instead of pasted code, no stub content.

## Plan-review resolutions

Must reflect the corrected transcript format from the cx2 ticket body (see epic claude-dev-tools-oaq comment, 2026-05-22): attribution on assistant messages only, episodes delimited by transcript position, `tool_result.is_error` as the error signal, `turn_duration` duration summing, and the rename-alias map.

## Instructions

1. Create `docs/MONITORING.md`.
2. Document the data source: `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<uuid>.jsonl`, the event types, the `attributionPlugin`/`attributionSkill` fields (assistant-message-only, plugin-namespaced), and that `tool_result` blocks live inside user messages.
3. Document Phase 1: how to run `scripts/analyze-sessions.py`, where output lands (`output/session-analysis/`), the episode-delimiting rule (un-tagged lines assigned to the open episode by position), the rename-alias map, and the friction-score formula. The formula and field semantics in the doc MUST match the script exactly.
4. Document Phase 2 — the Claude-in-the-loop judging workflow: the user runs the script, then asks Claude Code to judge the sampled episode slices using the rubric (trigger-appropriate, followed-instructions, task-completed, user-accepted; 1-5 + rationale); verdicts written to `output/session-analysis/verdicts/`. State explicitly this uses the Claude subscription and needs no API key. Note the heuristic Tier A correction signal is fuzzy and the Phase 2 judge is the authority on whether a correction actually occurred.
5. Document the false-negative pass: sampling no-attribution sessions and checking them against plugin descriptions.
6. Point to the script rather than pasting it.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docs/MONITORING.md` exists with data source, Phase 1, Phase 2, and false-negative sections
- [ ] All referenced paths and commands resolve to real files
- [ ] Friction formula and field semantics in the doc match the script implementation
- [ ] Episode-delimiting rule and rename-alias map are documented
- [ ] No pasted script body; uses a file pointer
