title: Build scripts/analyze-sessions.py session indexer
description:
## Description

Phase 1 indexer: a pure-mechanical, offline Python script that scans Claude Code session transcripts and emits per-skill-episode friction and outcome scoring. No API calls. Stdlib only; streaming line-by-line parse.

## Plan-review resolutions

Transcript-format assumptions were verified against live sessions and corrected below (see epic claude-dev-tools-oaq comment, 2026-05-22). Attribution fields live ONLY on assistant messages; tool_results live inside user messages; turn_duration is an un-attributed system event.

## Instructions

1. Create `scripts/analyze-sessions.py` (Python 3, stdlib only). Parse each JSONL file line-by-line (streaming) — never load a whole file into memory.
2. Discover marketplace plugins by scanning `plugins/*/` directory names. Maintain an explicit rename-alias map (at least `complexity-review`->`project-review`) so historical attribution for renamed plugins is not lost.
3. Walk all `~/.claude/projects/*/` directories; parse every `*.jsonl` file (one JSON object per line; ignore unparseable lines and unknown keys gracefully).
4. Identify "skill episodes": `attributionPlugin`/`attributionSkill` appear ONLY on `assistant` messages and are plugin-namespaced (e.g. `beads-tasks:beads-core`). Define an episode as a maximal run of assistant messages sharing the same `attributionSkill`, walking the transcript in line order. Assign interleaved un-tagged `user` messages, `tool_result` blocks (which live inside user-type messages), and `system` events to the currently-open episode by transcript position. Keep only episodes whose plugin resolves (after the rename-alias map) to a current marketplace plugin.
5. Compute Tier A friction signals per episode:
   - tool errors: `tool_result.is_error == true` (content block inside a user message) — Claude Code's canonical failure signal; it already covers Bash non-zero exits. Do NOT look for a separate `exitCode` field.
   - interruptions: `toolUseResult.interrupted == true`.
   - user corrections: follow-up user prompts whose first sentence matches a word-boundary regex for no/wrong/stop/don't/actually/revert — a weak signal, low weight.
   - permission denials; retries (repeated tool+input); turn count.
   - `AskUserQuestion` tool-use count.
   - duration: sum `durationMs` of `{"type":"system","subtype":"turn_duration"}` lines within the episode's line range (attributed by position).
6. Compute Tier B outcome signals: ended in commit/PR; tests run/passed where detectable.
7. Compute a friction score (weighted penalties normalized by turn count) — 0=smooth, higher=rockier. Keep correction-detection weight low.
8. Distinguish trigger type per episode: "explicit" if a `Skill` tool-use whose normalized (namespace-stripped) skill name equals the episode's `attributionSkill` appears in the same or immediately-preceding assistant turn as the episode's first message; otherwise "ambient".
9. Write outputs to `output/session-analysis/`:
   - `dataset.json` — per-episode SUMMARY records only (scores, counts, IDs, plugin/skill, line ranges, trigger type); NO raw message content.
   - `summary.md` — per-skill aggregates keyed on the full namespaced `attributionSkill`, plus a section listing attributed plugins NOT matched to a current marketplace plugin (with episode counts).
   - `episodes/` — sanitized per-episode slice files for a selected sample (rocky episodes + random baseline) for the Phase 2 judge.
10. Sanitize slices: strip credential-looking strings (env values, tokens, key-like strings) and cap large tool outputs. Never write outside `output/session-analysis/`.
11. Ship a tiny synthetic fixture JSONL (e.g. under `scripts/fixtures/`) with a known set of episodes, errors, and corrections, plus the expected indexer output, so acceptance review can diff actual vs expected.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Running the script on real local sessions produces `dataset.json`, `summary.md`, and `episodes/` slices
- [ ] Running the script on the bundled fixture produces the expected episode count, friction scores, and trigger types (diffable, not eyeballed)
- [ ] Episodes scoped to current marketplace plugins; rename-alias map applied; `summary.md` lists unmatched attributed plugins
- [ ] Episode delimiting assigns un-tagged user/tool/system lines to the open episode by position (tool_results and corrections are counted)
- [ ] Friction score and trigger type present per episode
- [ ] Script uses stdlib only, streams files line-by-line, and handles malformed/unknown JSONL lines without crashing

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Completed. scripts/analyze-sessions.py + scripts/fixtures/ (fixture jsonl, expected json, check-fixture.py). Real-session run: 129 episodes across 16 skills, 31 explicit triggers; dataset.json/summary.md/episodes/ produced cleanly. Fixture check: 2 episodes pass diff. Commit e5d95b9. output/ not tracked (gitignored). Not pushed.

title: Session-analysis monitoring for plugin usage
description:
## Outcome

A repeatable workflow to find Claude Code sessions where this repo's plugins/skills were used, and evaluate how well they performed. Mechanical scoring via a Python script; quality judging done by Claude Code itself (runs on the subscription — no API key).

## Background

Claude Code stores every session as JSONL under `~/.claude/projects/<project-slug>/<session-uuid>.jsonl`. Each assistant/user message carries `attributionPlugin` and `attributionSkill` fields identifying the active plugin/skill. This makes it possible to locate and evaluate plugin usage across all projects on the machine.

## Success criteria

- A script locates skill episodes (contiguous runs of messages tagged with one skill) across all `~/.claude/projects/*/` sessions, scoped to this repo's marketplace plugins.
- Mechanical friction + outcome scoring is produced per episode.
- `docs/MONITORING.md` documents the data source, the script, the friction formula, and the Claude-in-the-loop quality-judging workflow.
- `AGENTS.md` routes to the new doc; `output/` is gitignored.

## Design decisions

- Scope: all 11 projects on the machine, not just this repo.
- Tier C judge runs as a Claude Code workflow (subscription), NOT a standalone API script — no API key.
- Output folder: `output/session-analysis/` (whole `output/` gitignored).

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
Plan-review gate (reviewer plan-grilling, 2026-05-22). Grill sheet verified plan assumptions against ~150 live session JSONL files. 5 blocking + 6 non-blocking questions. Resolutions (orchestrator, autonomous per /goal directive):

Q1 SCOPING (blocking): Scope to current plugins/*/ set + maintain rename-alias map (complexity-review->project-review) so historical episodes survive renames; summary.md lists unmatched attributed plugins. ACCEPTED.
Q2 EPISODE DEF (blocking): attribution is on assistant messages only. Episode = maximal run of same-attributionSkill assistant messages; un-tagged user/tool_result/system lines assigned to open episode by transcript position. ACCEPTED.
Q3 ERROR SIGNAL (blocking): Use tool_result.is_error (content block) as canonical error signal; it already covers Bash non-zero exits. Drop the invented exitCode field. Use toolUseResult.interrupted for interruptions. ACCEPTED.
Q4 DURATION (blocking): turn_duration system events carry no attribution; sum durationMs over the episode line range, attributed by position. ACCEPTED.
Q5/Q6 TRIGGER+NAMESPACE (non-blocking): explicit-trigger = Skill tool-use whose normalized name == episode attributionSkill in same/prior assistant turn; aggregates key on full namespaced attributionSkill. ACCEPTED.
Q7 FIXTURE (blocking): cx2 must ship a synthetic fixture JSONL + expected output so c26 can diff, not eyeball. ACCEPTED.
Q8 CORRECTIONS (non-blocking): word-boundary regex on first sentence; low weight. ACCEPTED.
Q9 SANITIZATION (non-blocking): minimal — strip credential-looking strings, cap large tool outputs. ACCEPTED.
Q10 FOLD 39a INTO lm5 (non-blocking): DECLINED — keep 39a as a separate task; it preserves the approved epic structure and is trivial to execute.
Q11 OUTPUT SIZE (non-blocking): stream line-by-line; dataset.json holds summary records only, no raw message content. ACCEPTED.

cx2 and lm5 ticket bodies updated to reflect the corrected transcript format. Review gate cleared.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Epic closed. All 3 implementation tasks (cx2, lm5, 39a) closed; acceptance-review gate c26 passed and closed. Deliverables: scripts/analyze-sessions.py + scripts/fixtures/ (commit e5d95b9), docs/MONITORING.md (31759bd), AGENTS.md route + .gitignore output/ (6200b88). One non-blocking follow-up: claude-dev-tools-gac. Work committed on master, not pushed.

title: Add consistency-review skill: adversarial pattern + naming divergence reviewer
description:
## Description
Add a `consistency-review` skill to the `project-review` plugin. It reviews
whether a codebase feels like it was written by one person — the same job done
two different ways — in an adversarial, challenging style.

## Instructions
1. Create `plugins/project-review/skills/consistency-review/SKILL.md`.
2. Frontmatter `description`: positive trigger phrases (e.g. "review my codebase
   for consistency", "are we doing this two different ways?") PLUS a negative
   clause naming siblings — e.g. "Does not apply to over-engineering review (use
   complexity-review), directory layout (use structure-review), test review (use
   test-review), or pure formatting (use a linter)." Both user- and
   model-invocable.
3. Self-contained: inline the adversarial stance in SKILL.md; no cross-skill or
   shared-file dependency.
4. SKILL.md must contain the four concrete elements: numbered interrogation
   procedure, recommended-answer-per-question rule, explore-first instruction,
   explicit read-only contract.
5. The review must challenge, not catalogue. Cover at least:
   - Competing patterns for one concern — two HTTP clients, two error-handling
     styles, two config loaders.
   - Uneven naming conventions (getUser vs fetch_account vs loadProfile).
   - Inconsistent API/function shapes across siblings.
   - Import/module conventions, file-naming casing.
   - Do NOT relitigate pure formatting — that belongs to linters.
6. Baseline rule: prefer a documented convention (AGENTS.md / CODING.md) when one
   exists; otherwise flag minority deviations from the dominant pattern. Avoid
   "fixing" the majority to match a documented-but-ignored standard without
   surfacing the conflict.
7. Follow `docs/CODING.md` conventions; add `references/` only if needed.

## Files to Modify
- Create: `plugins/project-review/skills/consistency-review/SKILL.md` (+ references if needed)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `consistency-review` skill exists with valid frontmatter, both-invocable
- [ ] `description` has positive triggers AND a sibling-disambiguation negative clause
- [ ] SKILL.md contains all four concrete elements
- [ ] Covers competing patterns, naming, API shapes; excludes pure formatting
- [ ] Uses documented-convention-else-dominant-pattern baseline rule
- [ ] Self-contained — no cross-skill or shared-file dependency
- [ ] Structural validation passes

title: Add structure-review skill: adversarial layout + boundaries reviewer
description:
## Description
Add a `structure-review` skill to the `project-review` plugin. It reviews how a
project is physically organized — file/directory placement — in an adversarial,
challenging style. It judges "is this organized well?" and routes architectural
design verdicts to the `complexity-review` skill.

## Instructions
1. Create `plugins/project-review/skills/structure-review/SKILL.md`.
2. Frontmatter `description`: positive trigger phrases (e.g. "review my project
   structure", "is this layout sane?") PLUS a negative clause naming siblings —
   e.g. "Does not apply to over-engineering/architecture review (use
   complexity-review), test review (use test-review), or pattern consistency
   (use consistency-review)." Both user- and model-invocable.
3. Self-contained: inline the adversarial stance in SKILL.md; no cross-skill or
   shared-file dependency.
4. SKILL.md must contain the four concrete elements: numbered interrogation
   procedure, recommended-answer-per-question rule, explore-first instruction,
   explicit read-only contract.
5. Scope is PHYSICAL placement only. Cover at least:
   - Misplaced files — things in the wrong directory.
   - God-files vs over-splitting — file/module granularity.
   - Dead or orphaned code and files.
   - Tree not matching how the project describes itself (AGENTS.md / docs).
6. Layering / module-boundary smells: structure-review may FLAG a smell
   (e.g. "module A reaches deep into B") but must explicitly hand the design
   verdict to `complexity-review` rather than judging it. State this hand-off as
   a one-line rule in the skill. Rule of thumb: if the fix is "move a file" it
   belongs here; if the fix is "redesign the boundary", route to
   complexity-review.
7. Follow `docs/CODING.md` conventions; add `references/` only if needed.

## Files to Modify
- Create: `plugins/project-review/skills/structure-review/SKILL.md` (+ references if needed)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `structure-review` skill exists with valid frontmatter, both-invocable
- [ ] `description` has positive triggers AND a sibling-disambiguation negative clause
- [ ] SKILL.md contains all four concrete elements
- [ ] Covers misplaced files, file granularity, dead code, tree-vs-docs mismatch
- [ ] Layering smells are flagged-and-routed to complexity-review, not judged
- [ ] Self-contained — no cross-skill or shared-file dependency
- [ ] Structural validation passes

title: Add test-review skill: adversarial test quality + coverage reviewer
description:
## Description
Add a `test-review` skill to the `project-review` plugin. It reviews a project's
test setup for quality and coverage adequacy in an adversarial, challenging
style — it interrogates the user rather than reporting findings.

## Instructions
1. Create `plugins/project-review/skills/test-review/SKILL.md`.
2. Frontmatter `description`: positive trigger phrases (e.g. "review my tests",
   "are these tests any good?", "test coverage review") PLUS a negative clause
   naming siblings — e.g. "Does not apply to over-engineering review (use
   complexity-review), directory layout (use structure-review), or pattern
   consistency (use consistency-review)." Leave the skill both user- and
   model-invocable.
3. The skill is self-contained: inline the adversarial stance directly in
   SKILL.md. Do NOT load or depend on another skill or a shared file.
4. SKILL.md must contain these four concrete elements:
   - an explicit "interrogation procedure" section with sequential numbered
     questions;
   - a stated rule that each question carries a recommended answer;
   - a stated "explore the codebase before asking" instruction;
   - an explicit read-only contract (challenge and recommend, never edit code).
5. The review must challenge, not catalogue. Cover at least:
   - Speed: unit tests must run fast (not minutes); slow suites are guilty.
   - Long-running tests must justify their cost — what do they buy?
   - Coverage adequacy: what matters that is untested — not raw % chasing.
   - Test quality: brittle assertions, over-mocking, tests that cannot fail,
     tests asserting implementation instead of behaviour.
6. Follow plugin/skill conventions in `docs/CODING.md`; add `references/` only if
   the SKILL.md would otherwise be too long.

## Files to Modify
- Create: `plugins/project-review/skills/test-review/SKILL.md` (+ references if needed)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `test-review` skill exists with valid frontmatter, both-invocable
- [ ] `description` has positive triggers AND a sibling-disambiguation negative clause
- [ ] SKILL.md contains all four concrete elements (numbered interrogation procedure, recommended-answer rule, explore-first instruction, read-only contract)
- [ ] Covers test speed, long-test justification, coverage adequacy, test quality
- [ ] Self-contained — no cross-skill or shared-file dependency
- [ ] Structural validation passes

title: Rename complexity-review plugin to project-review
description:
## Description
Rename the `complexity-review` plugin directory and identity to `project-review`,
convert it to a skills-only plugin, and create a RULES.md capturing the
multi-reviewer design decisions. The existing `complexity-review` *skill* is
left completely untouched.

## Instructions
1. Rename `plugins/complexity-review/` -> `plugins/project-review/`.
2. Update `plugins/project-review/.claude-plugin/plugin.json`: set `name` to
   `project-review`, refresh `description` and `keywords` for a multi-reviewer
   plugin. Do NOT change the `version` field.
3. Update the root `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` entry: name, description,
   keywords, and source path. Do NOT touch any `version` field — a partial bump
   breaks `scripts/check-internal-consistency.py` Check B.
4. Delete `commands/` — the plugin is skills-only; the `complexity-review` skill
   is already both user- and model-invocable.
5. Do NOT rewire or modify the existing `complexity-review` skill. It keeps its
   structured verdict-report format; it is NOT converted to interrogation style.
   `skills/complexity-review/` must stay byte-for-byte unchanged.
6. Rewrite `README.md` for the renamed plugin and the multi-reviewer scope
   (four skills: complexity-review + the three new reviewers).
7. Create `plugins/project-review/RULES.md` capturing the non-derivable design
   decisions: (a) skills-only, no commands — the `/complexity-review` slash
   command was deliberately dropped; (b) the four-skill division of labour and
   hand-off rules (structure-review flags layering smells but routes the design
   verdict to complexity-review); (c) complexity-review keeps its verdict-report
   format and is NOT interrogation-style; (d) each new reviewer skill is
   self-contained — the adversarial stance is inlined per skill, there is no
   shared reference file and no `skills/_shared/` directory.
8. Fix all repo-wide references to the old plugin name AND the
   `/complexity-review` command: marketplace JSON, `docs/` (OVERVIEW.md and
   others), `AGENTS.md` routing, READMEs.

## Files to Modify
- Rename: `plugins/complexity-review/` -> `plugins/project-review/`

- Delete: `plugins/project-review/commands/`
- Create: `plugins/project-review/RULES.md`
- Do NOT modify: `skills/complexity-review/` contents

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `commands/` removed; plugin is skills-only

title: [epic] Expand complexity-review into a multi-reviewer project-review plugin
description:
## Description
Rename the `complexity-review` plugin to `project-review` and grow it into a
home for multiple adversarial review skills. Today it ships one skill
(`complexity-review`). This epic adds three more reviewers, each written in the
same skeptical, challenging style: they interrogate the user (grill-me style —
sequential questions, recommended answers, explore the codebase instead of
asking when possible) rather than politely listing findings.

## Outcome
- The plugin is named `project-review` and loads under that name; the existing
  `complexity-review` skill keeps its name and still triggers.
- The plugin ships four skills: `complexity-review`, `test-review`,
  `structure-review`, `consistency-review`.
- Each new skill is a single self-contained skill, both user- and
  model-invocable. No `commands/`, no thin wrapper skills.
- All new reviewers are read-only: they challenge and recommend, never edit code.

## Design notes
- Skills-only plugin (drop `commands/`), following the user's preference.
- The adversarial stance should live as a shared reference all four skills can
  point at, to avoid drift — the rename task establishes it; reviewer tasks
  consume it.

## Out of scope
- Version bump / release — separate RELEASING.md flow after the epic closes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Plugin renamed to `project-review`; marketplace validation passes
- [ ] Three new adversarial reviewer skills present and valid
- [ ] complexity-review skill still discoverable and triggers

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
Plan-review gate — grill sheet resolved (reviewer agent). Decisions adopted (all per reviewer's recommended answers, grounded in repo conventions):

Q1/Q2 — No shared adversarial-stance reference and no skills/_shared/ directory (not a repo convention; a dir without SKILL.md is not a skill). Each new reviewer skill is self-contained: the adversarial stance is inlined into its own SKILL.md. Resolves the epic's internal tension between "self-contained" and "shared reference" in favour of self-containment.

Q3 — The existing complexity-review skill is NOT rewired or modified. It keeps its structured verdict-report format; it is not converted to grill-me interrogation style. 7dx instruction updated to "leave it untouched".

Q4 — 7dx edits marketplace.json for the rename (name/description/keywords/source path) but changes NO version field anywhere. A partial version bump breaks check-internal-consistency.py Check B. Version bump stays in the separate RELEASING flow.

Q5 — "complexity-review still triggers" is verified structurally, not at runtime: SKILL.md frontmatter unchanged + structural validation passes + no stale refs. Live trigger-testing is a manual post-merge check. cub criterion rephrased accordingly.

Q6 — structure-review covers PHYSICAL placement only. Layering/boundary smells are flagged-and-routed to complexity-review, not judged. 24r updated with the hand-off rule.

Q7 — Each new skill's description carries positive triggers PLUS a negative clause naming its sibling skills (repo's existing disambiguation pattern). Added to og0/24r/rw0.

Q8 — 7dx creates plugins/project-review/RULES.md capturing the non-derivable design decisions (skills-only/command dropped; four-skill division of labour + hand-off rules; complexity-review keeps verdict format; skills self-contained).

Q9 — Deleting commands/ (the /complexity-review slash command) is intentional and confirmed; recorded in RULES.md. Repo-wide reference cleanup must cover /complexity-review command mentions, not just the plugin name.

Q10 — "adversarial/interrogative" acceptance criteria made testable: each new SKILL.md must contain (a) a numbered interrogation procedure, (b) a recommended-answer-per-question rule, (c) an explore-the-codebase-first instruction, (d) an explicit read-only contract. Added to og0/24r/rw0 ACs.

Q11 — marketplace/plugin.json email vs git-config email inconsistency is OUT OF SCOPE; filed as a separate chore.

Reviewer also confirmed the dependency graph is correct (no change needed).

title: Build the explore-project skill, references, and RULES.md
description:
## Description
Build the complete content of the project-explore plugin: the `explore-project` orchestrator skill (with INLINE research — no sub-agent, per plan-review Q1), its two reference files, and the plugin RULES.md.

## Instructions
1. Create `plugins/project-explore/skills/explore-project/SKILL.md` (user-invocable). Workflow:
   - **Phase 0 — beads check + epic.** Detect whether beads is initialised (`.beads/` present and `bd` usable). If not, stop with guidance and do nothing else. Otherwise create the exploration epic ("Explore <project> — <date>").
   - **Phase 1 — research INLINE** (no sub-agent). Read canonical docs (project-docs taxonomy: AGENTS.md routing, docs/OVERVIEW.md, etc.) and history/intent (beads tickets open + recently closed, GitHub issues via `gh`, end-user docs, changelog / recent commits). Write a project-understanding file to a throwaway temp path (`mktemp`). Track this with a research task opened and closed under the epic.
   - **Phase 2 — exploration loop, ONE action at a time.** pick (verify a feature / walk a flow / try to break something) -> do (launch the product using launch patterns described INLINE in the skill — CLI, server, TUI, browser, library; do NOT depend on the harness `run` skill) -> judge (3 questions: expected? OK for a real user? what would improve it?) -> file a finding or question task -> check in with the user (continue / redirect / stop). Findings: beads type `bug` + label `explore:finding`. Questions: beads type `task` + label `explore:question`. Both as children of the exploration epic. Before filing, dedup against open epic children (title/text check). Mutation safety: ask at session start whether a scratch/dev env exists and prefer it; any write/delete/send action requires explicit per-action confirmation before running; volume tests count as destructive. Escape hatch: user may say "do next N without asking" — suspends check-ins for non-destructive actions only; destructive actions still force a stop-and-confirm.
   - **Phase 3 — wrap-up.** Post a summary plus an explicit coverage-and-limits note on the epic. Never declare "all good".
2. Create `references/understanding-template.md` — schema with six sections: What it is / Implemented features / Known-or-likely-fragile areas / User flows discovered / Expectations to verify / General context & history.
3. Create `references/break-it.md` — the "try to break it" instincts (extreme input, volume, order/interruption, empty/first-run, unhappy path, consistency), framed as examples, not a checklist.
4. Create `plugins/project-explore/RULES.md` recording the locked decisions (research inline, temp understanding file, beads issue types + labels, mutation-safety rule, dedup, escape-hatch boundary, no run-skill dependency, beads-required Phase-0 check). Follow docs/CODING.md for RULES.md format.
5. Do NOT add a `dependencies` field to plugin.json.

## Files to Modify
- create `plugins/project-explore/skills/explore-project/SKILL.md`
- create `plugins/project-explore/skills/explore-project/references/understanding-template.md`
- create `plugins/project-explore/skills/explore-project/references/break-it.md`
- create `plugins/project-explore/RULES.md`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SKILL.md implements Phases 0-3; Phase 0 stops cleanly with guidance when beads is absent
- [ ] Phase 1 research is inline — no agent file referenced; understanding file written to a temp path
- [ ] Loop does one action then checks in; findings filed as `bug`+`explore:finding`, questions as `task`+`explore:question`, both children of the exploration epic
- [ ] `understanding-template.md` has all six sections; `break-it.md` present and framed as instincts/examples
- [ ] `RULES.md` records every locked decision listed in instruction 4
- [ ] plugin.json has no `dependencies` field
- [ ] `bash tests/run-all.sh` passes with the plugin present

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Done by tasker. Created SKILL.md (Phases 0-3, inline research, mktemp temp file, one-action loop with mutation-safety/dedup/escape-hatch), references/understanding-template.md (6 sections), references/break-it.md (instincts as examples), RULES.md (8 locked decisions). Removed unused agents/ dir + .gitkeep files; updated README.md. Validation: tests/run-all.sh 2 passed 0 failed; check-internal-consistency CHECK A+B PASS; plugin.json has no dependencies field. All acceptance criteria met.

title: Resolve open design questions & validate scope for project-explore
description:
## Description
The project-explore design (private/research-explore.html, §7) has unresolved questions a tasker must not improvise. Resolve them with the user and record the decisions as comments on epic claude-dev-tools-t7z before the agent/skill tasks become executable.

## Open Questions
- [ ] Understanding-file lifetime: throwaway temp file, or kept (attached to the research task / a scratch dir) for human review?
- [ ] Finding vs. question: which beads issue types to use (e.g. bug for findings, question/task for questions) — check what beads-tasks supports.
- [ ] Mutation safety: how the loop avoids destructive actions — scratch/dev environment preference, confirm-before-destructive rule.
- [ ] Dedup: should the loop check open epic tasks before filing a new finding/question?
- [ ] Escape hatch for "one action then ask": allow the user to say "do the next N without asking".

## Scope validation
- [ ] Run a complexity-review pass: is a full plugin + sub-agent warranted, or is something lighter sufficient? Record the verdict.

## Why This Needs Discussion
These decisions shape the research agent (T3) and the orchestrator skill (T4). Implementation cannot start until they are settled.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Every open question above resolved and recorded as a comment on epic claude-dev-tools-t7z
- [ ] complexity-review verdict recorded
- [ ] needs:discussion label removed and task unblocked

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter

title: Build the project-explore plugin (beads-driven exploratory testing)
description:
## Outcome
A new marketplace plugin, `project-explore`, that performs assisted exploratory testing of a project:

1. Opens a beads epic for the exploration session.
2. Researches the project INLINE (Phase 1 of the orchestrator skill — no separate sub-agent): reads docs and history and writes a structured project-understanding file to a temp path.
3. The orchestrator skill reads that file and exercises the product one action at a time, checking in with the user between each action.
4. Files a beads task for every finding (type `bug`, label `explore:finding`) and every question (type `task`, label `explore:question`), as children of the exploration epic.

## Locked decisions
- Ships as a dedicated new plugin `plugins/project-explore/` (not a project-ops skill).
- Research is done inline by the orchestrator skill — no project-researcher sub-agent (plan-review Q1).
- Understanding file is a throwaway temp file (plan-review Q4).
- Beads-required: implemented as a Phase-0 runtime check; if beads is not initialised the skill stops. No `dependencies` field in plugin.json (Q9).
- The orchestrator checks in with the user after every action; a "do next N" escape hatch suspends check-ins for non-destructive actions only (Q7).

## Reference design
`private/research-explore.html` (design v2). Full plan-review decisions recorded as a comment on this epic.

## Success criteria
- The plugin is installable and passes structural validation (plugin-dev:plugin-validator + tests/run-all.sh).
- The explore-project skill runs the epic + inline research + one-action loop and files finding/question tasks.
- RULES.md records the locked decisions; plugin registered in marketplace.json and the README table.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
Plan-review decisions (grill sheet on wx9), resolved with the user. These are the locked design decisions for the epic:

Q1 [user]: Research is folded INLINE into the orchestrator skill — NO separate project-researcher sub-agent. Task c9x is dropped; the understanding-file template moves into unr.
Q4 [user]: The project-understanding file is a throwaway temp file (mktemp), not kept.
Q3: Findings -> beads type `bug` + label `explore:finding`. Questions -> beads type `task` + label `explore:question`. No custom beads types (bd has no `question` type).
Q5: Mutation safety — orchestrator asks at session start whether a scratch/dev environment exists and prefers it; any action that writes/deletes/sends gets explicit per-action confirmation BEFORE running; volume tests ("hundreds of entries") count as destructive.
Q6: Dedup — before filing, list open epic children and do a title/text check; if a likely duplicate exists, comment on it instead of creating a new task. Keep lightweight.
Q7: Escape hatch — "do next N without asking" suspends check-ins for NON-destructive actions only; destructive actions (Q5) always force a stop-and-confirm even mid-batch.
Q8: The `run` skill is a harness built-in, not a depend-able plugin — the orchestrator describes launch patterns inline and must be self-contained.
Q9: "beads-required" is implemented purely as a Phase-0 runtime check (.beads present / bd usable, else stop with guidance). No `dependencies` field in plugin.json.
Q10: Add plugins/project-explore/RULES.md recording the locked decisions — a deliverable of unr.
Q11: No tests/ smoke suite (plugin ships no scripts). w6k reframed to: confirm marketplace registration, run tests/run-all.sh + check-internal-consistency.py, run plugin-dev:plugin-validator.
Q12: Acceptance criteria on unr tightened (see updated task body).
Q13: Epic consolidated — 888 done, c9x dropped; remaining implementation is unr only, then w6k, then bex.
Q2: moot — 888 was already completed before the dependency could be added.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text: Epic complete. All implementation tasks and the acceptance-review gate (bex) closed. All three epic success criteria met: plugin installable + structural validation green; explore-project skill implements the epic/inline-research/one-action-loop with finding & question task filing; RULES.md records the 8 locked decisions; plugin registered in marketplace.json + README. project-explore plugin delivered at plugins/project-explore/ (v1.11.0). Not committed — changes are in the working tree.

title: Add 'project-' prefix to all user-invocable skills in project-ops and project-docs
description:
## Goal

Prefix every user-invocable skill name with 'project-' so the slash-command surface is namespaced consistently (e.g. /project-ops:project-executes-tests).

## Scope

project-ops (untracked/unreleased, 3 skills): analyze-monitoring-data, executes-tests, trigger-release.
project-docs (released v1.10.0, 5 user-invocable skills): create-docs, improve-doc, init-or-update-docs, review-docs, revise-docs. The 'project-docs' skill itself is user-invocable:false and is NOT renamed.

title: Update beads-core references to cite scripts instead of restating checks in prose
description:

Update `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/references/ticket-rules.md` §Ticket readiness checklist — keep the 4 named checks but add a leading line: "These checks are implemented by `scripts/ticket-check.py <id>` — agents should run that and act on the JSON output instead of re-deriving the checks inline."
2. Update `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/references/execution-orchestration.md` §Step 1 (Plan-review gate) — point at `scripts/plan-review-gate.py` for the deterministic part of the gate check. The reviewer-grilling logic stays in prose (it's agent-judgment).

4. Update `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/references/work-intake.md` §2B — point at `scripts/bd-inventory.py` for the bulk discovery (in_progress / ready / blocked grouping). Agent still narrates the proposal to the user.

title: Add verify.sh — orchestrator over ticket-check, plan-review-gate, epic-gate
description:
## Description
Create `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/scripts/verify.sh` — mirror `project-docs/skills/project-docs/scripts/verify.sh`. Composes T2/T3/T4 into a single PASS/FAIL summary for a given epic.

## Instructions
1. Create `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/scripts/verify.sh`.
2. CLI: `verify.sh <epic-id>`. Exit 0 if all hard checks pass; 1 otherwise.
3. Run, in order:
   - **Check 1**: `plan-review-gate.py <epic-id>` — hard fail if gate is `needs-answers`
   - **Check 2**: for each open child task of the epic, run `ticket-check.py <task-id>` — hard fail if any returns FAIL
   - **Check 3**: `epic-gate.py <epic-id>` — soft warning unless epic is in a state where closure is expected (e.g., all children closed); the orchestrator decides when to invoke
4. Print section headers and a final summary line: `=== VERIFY: PASS ===` or `=== VERIFY: FAIL (<n> hard check(s) failed) ===`.
5. Pattern after `project-docs/scripts/verify.sh` for orchestration style (helpers, sections, hard-fail counter).
6. Support `-h|--help`.

## Files to Modify
- create: `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/scripts/verify.sh`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Script is executable (`chmod +x`)
- [ ] `verify.sh <some-epic-id>` runs all three checks and exits non-zero if any hard check fails
- [ ] Output format matches `project-docs/verify.sh` (=== Check N ===, RESULT: PASS/FAIL, final summary)
- [ ] Script does not invoke any bd write commands directly (only via the sub-scripts, which are also read-only)
close_reason: Superseded — see parent epic claude-dev-tools-hao comment dated 2026-05-19 for full rationale. Net: bd already provides the underlying capability for each of these scripts; the epic's project-docs analogy did not transfer.

title: Add plan-review-gate.py — plan-grilling evidence check
description:
## Description
Create `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/scripts/plan-review-gate.py` — encode Step 1 of `execution-orchestration.md` as a script. Determines whether the plan-review gate has been satisfied for an epic.

## Instructions
1. Create `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/scripts/plan-review-gate.py`.
2. CLI: `plan-review-gate.py <epic-id> [--format=json|text]`. Exit 0 on `clean`, 1 on `needs-answers`, 2 on bad invocation.
3. Check (all read-only):
   - **need:review label** — if still present on the epic → gate is `needs-answers`
   - **grill-sheet comments** — search epic comments for matches of `^Q\d+:` (per reviewer.md grill-sheet format)
   - **unanswered Qs** — heuristic: for each `Q\d+:` comment, check whether a sibling comment containing `Answer:` or starting `A\d+:` exists; if not → `needs-answers`
4. JSON output: `{epic_id, gate: "clean"|"needs-answers", grill_questions: [{q_number, present: bool, answered: bool, comment_id?}], summary}`.
5. Add docstring at the top.

## Files to Modify
- create: `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/scripts/plan-review-gate.py`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `python3 plan-review-gate.py <epic-id>` returns valid JSON
- [ ] Running against an epic that still carries `need:review` returns `needs-answers`
- [ ] Running against an epic with no `need:review` label and no grill comments returns `clean`
- [ ] Detail text identifies which Q numbers (if any) are unanswered
- [ ] Script does not invoke any bd write commands

title: Add epic-gate.py — epic closure gate check
description:
## Description
Create `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/scripts/epic-gate.py` — verify an epic is ready to close per `beads-acceptance-review.md` §Epic closure rule and `execution-orchestration.md` §Closure order.

## Instructions
1. Create `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/scripts/epic-gate.py`.
2. CLI: `epic-gate.py <epic-id> [--format=json|text]`. Exit 0 on PASS, 1 on FAIL, 2 on bad invocation.
3. Check (all read-only via `bd show`, `bd dep list`):
   - **child closure** — all child implementation tasks of the epic are closed
   - **acceptance review exists** — epic has a dependent task whose title starts with `Acceptance Review:`
   - **acceptance review closed** — that task's status is `closed`
   - **no open critical follow-ups** — no open issues whose title starts with `Found:` and priority is P0 or P1 referencing this epic (search comments or descriptions for the epic ID)
   - **need:review removed** — the epic does not carry the `need:review` label
4. JSON output: `{epic_id, status: "pass"|"fail", checks: [{name, result, detail}], summary}`.
5. Add docstring at the top.

## Files to Modify
- create: `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/scripts/epic-gate.py`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `python3 epic-gate.py <epic-id>` returns valid JSON
- [ ] Running against an in-progress epic with open children returns FAIL listing the open children
- [ ] Running against a closed/complete epic returns PASS
- [ ] Script does not invoke any bd write commands
- [ ] Detail text on failures is specific enough that the orchestrator can act without re-querying
close_reason: Superseded — see parent epic claude-dev-tools-hao comment dated 2026-05-19 for full rationale. Net: bd already provides the underlying capability for each of these scripts; the epic's project-docs analogy did not transfer.

title: Add ticket-check.py — ticket readiness checklist
description:
## Description
Create `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/scripts/ticket-check.py` — encode the readiness checklist from `beads-core/references/ticket-rules.md` as a script. Currently the tasker, verifier, and reviewer each re-derive these checks in prose.

## Instructions
1. Create `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/scripts/ticket-check.py`.
2. CLI: `ticket-check.py <ticket-id> [--format=json|text]`. Exit 0 on PASS, 1 on FAIL, 2 on bad invocation.
3. Read the ticket via `bd show <id>` (read-only).
4. Implement these checks (mirror `ticket-rules.md` §Ticket readiness checklist):
   - **labels** — fail if labels include `needs:discussion` or `has:open-questions`
   - **status** — fail if status is `blocked`
   - **sections** — fail if the description is missing `## Description`, `## Instructions` (only for type=task), or `## Acceptance Criteria` headings
   - **acceptance criteria** — fail if no `- [ ]` checkboxes in the Acceptance Criteria section
   - **orphan comments** (soft warning, not fail) — flag comments that look like decisions/scope changes if a heuristic match (e.g., lines starting with "Decision:" or "Scope:") is not echoed in the description
5. JSON output: `{ticket_id, status: "pass"|"fail", checks: [{name, result: "pass"|"fail"|"warn", detail, suggested_comment_text?}], summary}`.
6. For every FAIL, include a `suggested_comment_text` field with tracker-ready short comment text the caller can paste into `bd comments add`.
7. Add docstring at the top.

## Files to Modify
- create: `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/scripts/ticket-check.py`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `python3 ticket-check.py <some-open-task-id>` returns valid JSON
- [ ] Running against a ticket missing the Acceptance Criteria section returns FAIL with a suggested_comment_text
- [ ] Running against a clean executable task returns PASS
- [ ] Script does not invoke any bd write commands
- [ ] Comment text follows the format rules in `ticket-rules.md` (short, decision-oriented, one finding per comment)
close_reason: Superseded — see parent epic claude-dev-tools-hao comment dated 2026-05-19 for full rationale. Net: bd already provides the underlying capability for each of these scripts; the epic's project-docs analogy did not transfer.

title: Add bd-inventory.py — structured tracker snapshot
description:
## Description
Create `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/scripts/bd-inventory.py` — a read-only structured snapshot of the beads tracker, modeled on `plugins/project-docs/skills/project-docs/scripts/inventory.py`. This is foundation work: T2/T3/T4 may shell out to it or share its JSON output conventions, so land it first to establish the shapes.

## Instructions
1. Create `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/scripts/bd-inventory.py`.
2. CLI: `bd-inventory.py [--format=json|text]`. Default JSON. Exit 0 on success, 1 on bad invocation.
3. Implement by shelling out to `bd` (read-only commands only: `bd list`, `bd show`, `bd ready`, `bd blocked`, `bd dep list`). Parse `bd`'s output formats — prefer JSON-emitting subcommands if `bd` supports them; otherwise text parsing is acceptable.
4. Output structure (JSON):
   - `epics`: list of `{id, title, status, labels, has_acceptance_review: bool, acceptance_review_id, child_task_ids, need_review_label_present}`
   - `in_progress`: list of `{id, title, latest_comment_snippet}` — used by work-intake.md §2B blocker check
   - `blocked`: list of `{id, title, reason_label}` grouped by `needs:discussion` / `has:open-questions` / other
   - `ready`: list of `{id, title, priority, type}`
   - `found_bugs`: list of `{id, title, priority, parent_task_ref}` — bugs whose title starts with `Found:`
   - `summary`: counts for each of the above
5. Implement `--format=text` for human-readable output mirroring `inventory.py`'s text formatter.
6. Add a docstring at the top explaining the contract.

## Files to Modify
- create: `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/scripts/bd-inventory.py`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Script is executable (`chmod +x`)
- [ ] `python3 bd-inventory.py` returns valid JSON parseable by `python3 -c "import json,sys; json.load(sys.stdin)"`
- [ ] `python3 bd-inventory.py --format=text` produces readable sections for each category
- [ ] Running against this repo's tracker correctly identifies the current in-progress epic (claude-dev-tools-1t9) and the new epic claude-dev-tools-hao
- [ ] Script does not invoke any bd write commands (`bd create`, `bd update`, `bd close`, `bd comments add`, `bd dep add`)
- [ ] Style matches `project-docs/scripts/inventory.py` (docstring header, helper sections, json/text dual-mode)
close_reason: Superseded — see parent epic claude-dev-tools-hao comment dated 2026-05-19 for full rationale. Net: bd already provides the underlying capability for each of these scripts; the epic's project-docs analogy did not transfer.

title: Add helper scripts to beads-tasks plugin (mirror project-docs pattern)
description:
## Description
Port the "deterministic checks as scripts with structured JSON output" pattern from `plugins/project-docs/skills/project-docs/scripts/` into `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/scripts/`. The beads workflows currently ask the orchestrator (and subagents) to run the same ticket-readiness, plan-review-gate, and epic-closure-gate checks in prose, over and over. Extract them into single-call scripts so prose references collapse from "do these eight steps" to "run `scripts/X`".

## Motivation
Project-docs cut a lot of token cost and prose drift by moving deterministic checks (inventory, link validation, file invariants) into scripts. The beads-tasks plugin has the same shape of repetitive deterministic checks scattered across `beads-core/references/`. Mirroring the pattern should produce comparable simplification.

## Scope
- New scripts under `plugins/beads-tasks/skills/beads-core/scripts/`
- Updates to `beads-core/references/*.md` to point at the scripts instead of restating the checks in prose
- README updates where relevant

Out of scope:
- Hook-based enforcement of the serialized-writes rule (different mechanism, separate work)
- Anything that requires agent judgment (reviewer grill-sheet generation, "are acceptance criteria really testable", orphaned-comment severity)
- Wrapping `bd create/update/close` themselves

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All child implementation tasks closed
- [ ] Acceptance review task complete
- [ ] Each new script has a documented `--format=json|text` interface where appropriate
- [ ] References in `beads-core/references/*.md` cite the scripts instead of restating the checks they encode
- [ ] `scripts/verify.sh <epic-id>` runs the composed gates end-to-end against a real epic in this repo
close_reason: Closed without execution after review. See epic comment dated 2026-05-19: the project-docs scripts pattern does not transfer to beads-tasks because bd is already a structured API. Single legitimate candidate (AR task creation) reopened as a fresh standalone task.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## Review verdict — epic closed without execution (2026-05-19)

Reviewer-agent grill returned `grill-status: needs-answers`. Load-bearing findings, verified empirically:

1. **bd is already the API.** `bd lint`, `bd epic status`, `bd epic close-eligible`, `bd query --json` cover ~80% of what the proposed scripts would do. The project-docs analogy (this epic's stated motivation) fails because project-docs scripts filled a real API gap; beads-tasks doesn't have that gap.
2. **`plan-review-gate.py` (T-3mp) encodes a convention that has never existed in this tracker.** Grepped `^Q\d+:` across closed epics sei/dw0/c0m/koh/yt7: 0 matches. Script would silently always return `clean`. Provably useless against this repo's actual practice.
3. **Internal contradiction in epic scope.** Epic body listed "orphaned-comment severity" as out-of-scope; T-wfw instructions step 4 included an orphan-comments check. Planning bug on my part.
4. **AR task (T-rzk) violated ticket-rules.md line 29.** Criterion "spot-check by skimming a recent epic" is the exact phrasing ticket-rules forbids ("'Works', 'is done', or 'looks correct' are not acceptable"). Self-referential failure.
5. **T-yjh designed in dual sources of truth.** Step 7 said "Do NOT remove the prose checks — leave them as the spec." Same drift hazard as open task claude-dev-tools-3oi.

## Honest re-scope

After re-examining every reference and agent file with the lens "where could a script REPLACE markdown (not augment it)":
- ticket-check / epic-gate / bd-inventory / verify.sh: rejected — bd already covers these
- plan-review-gate: rejected — encodes a non-existent convention
- Bug draft template: rejected — agents write freeform
- Serialized-writes / git-safety / delegation rules: rejected — policy, not procedure
- Planning / work intake: rejected — judgment, not procedure

**Single legitimate candidate:** AR task creation. Every epic requires a `Acceptance Review: <Epic Name>` task with 4 canonical AC checkboxes, linked parent-child to the epic. Identical body every time. Currently ~36 lines of heredoc template across planning.md (lines 50-73) and beads-acceptance-review.md (lines 31-42). A single script can subsume the template; the markdown collapses to one line.

Tracked as a fresh standalone task (no epic, no AR — one script + two doc edits).

title: Tighten CLAUDE.md rule: must be exactly 1 line, enforce in script + docs
description: Current rule allows 'existing content below is preserved' (project-setup.md:7, docs-init.md:26, docs-update.md:23). This permits the BEADS-block drift we just fixed. Tighten: CLAUDE.md is exactly @AGENTS.md (one line). Extra content is a bug. claude-md.sh check fails on extra content; claude-md.sh init refuses to clobber unless --rewrite; doc-init/doc-update workflows migrate content to AGENTS.md or topic docs before init.
close_reason: Tightened CLAUDE.md rule across script + skill workflow. claude-md.sh: check fails on extra content; init aborts (exit 2) on extra content; init --rewrite for destructive collapse after migration. Reference docs updated: project-setup.md, project-structure.md, project-docs-lifecycle.md, project-doc-review-guidelines.md, SKILL.md. Workflow refs (docs-init.md, docs-update.md, docs-revise.md) now include an explicit CLAUDE migration step that classifies extra content by destination (AGENTS.md/topic doc/.claude.local.md/delete) and seeks per-edit approval before collapsing. test-claude-md.sh rewritten (29 cases). All 4 test suites pass; verify.sh against this repo still PASS.

title: Self-eats-dogfood: apply project-docs guidelines to this repo's own docs
description: Run new guidelines (A1/A2/A4/A5 + injected-block rule) against AGENTS.md and docs/. Known issue: AGENTS.md has a BEADS INTEGRATION injected block (lines 31-77) that violates the new auto-injected-block rule.
close_reason:
Applied new project-docs review-guidelines to this repo's own docs. Originally-named issue (BEADS INTEGRATION injected block in AGENTS.md) was already fixed in 91bce38. Full re-review surfaced 5 BLOCKER + 14 MAJOR + 5 MINOR findings; fixed all 5 BLOCKERs in this pass:
- README.md: added keep-awake-linux row; replaced fabricated 'claude plugin install <url>' with marketplace flow ('/plugin marketplace add hanskohlreiter/claude-dev-tools' + '/plugin install <name>@claude-dev-tools'); corrected enabledPlugins example.

title: claude-md.sh: check/init drop final line when CLAUDE.md has no trailing newline
description:
## Description

`claude-md.sh check` returns "INVALID: first non-empty line is not @AGENTS.md (got: <empty>)" when CLAUDE.md contains `@AGENTS.md` with no trailing newline. Bash's `read` loop drops the final line when there is no `\n` terminator.

Discovered while running `verify.sh` against `/home/hans/dev/github/claude-dev-tools` (task claude-dev-tools-69b). This repo's own CLAUDE.md is 10 bytes — `@AGENTS.md` with no trailing newline — so `verify.sh` reports a false hard failure.

## Repro

```bash
mkdir /tmp/r && printf '@AGENTS.md' > /tmp/r/CLAUDE.md
plugins/project-docs/skills/project-docs/scripts/claude-md.sh check /tmp/r
# Actual: INVALID ... got: <empty>; exit 1
# Expected: OK; exit 0
```

## Root cause

The `check` (and likely `init`'s read path) uses a `while read` loop without the `|| [[ -n "$line" ]]` continuation pattern. POSIX `read` returns non-zero when it hits EOF without seeing the line terminator, which silently drops the last line if it lacks a trailing `\n`.

## Fix

In the file-scan loop in `claude-md.sh`, change `while IFS= read -r line; do … done < "$file"` to `while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do … done < "$file"`. Apply to both `init` and `check` code paths if both have the issue.

## Files to Modify

- `plugins/project-docs/skills/project-docs/scripts/claude-md.sh`
- `plugins/project-docs/skills/project-docs/scripts/tests/test-claude-md.sh` — add a regression test: CLAUDE.md created with `printf '@AGENTS.md'` (no trailing newline) must pass `check` and not be modified by `init`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `claude-md.sh check /tmp/r` (with `printf '@AGENTS.md'` content) returns OK, exit 0
- [ ] `claude-md.sh init /tmp/r` (same content) is a no-op (no rewrite)
- [ ] New regression test added to `test-claude-md.sh` covers the missing-trailing-newline case
- [ ] `verify.sh /home/hans/dev/github/claude-dev-tools` exits 0 (only soft warnings remain)
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
close_reason: Fixed: replaced 'while IFS= read -r line' with '... || [[ -n "$line" ]]' in first_nonempty_line and the test helper. Added regression test (3 assertions). All 14 tests pass; verify.sh exits 0 against real repo.

title: verify.sh: orchestrator running Phase 7 mandatory checks in one command
description:
## Description

Bash orchestrator that implements Phase 7 (Verify) of `references/project-docs-lifecycle.md` as a single command. Calls the other scripts and aggregates exit codes.

## Files to Create

- `plugins/project-docs/skills/project-docs/scripts/verify.sh` (executable, `#!/usr/bin/env bash`, `set -uo pipefail` — do NOT `set -e` so all checks run before exit)

## Instructions

1. CLI shape:
   - `verify.sh <repo-root>` — runs all checks, prints summary, exits non-zero if any hard check failed
2. Checks (in order):
   - `claude-md.sh check <repo-root>` — hard failure
   - `validate-routes.py <repo-root>` — hard failure on unresolved refs
   - `inventory.py <repo-root> --format=text` (or parse JSON) — emit soft warnings for: missing canonical docs, hollow docs, non-canonical docs (consolidation candidates), location violations
3. Output format: a short header per check, the check's output (or summary), and a final pass/fail line
4. Exit codes:
   - 0: all hard checks pass (soft warnings are non-fatal)
   - 1: any hard check failed
5. Script resolves sibling-script paths relative to its own location (use `$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")` pattern), so it works from any CWD

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Script exists and is executable
- [ ] Runs all checks even when an early one fails (no short-circuit)
- [ ] Exit 0 on a clean fixture
- [ ] Exit non-zero when CLAUDE.md is malformed or any route is unresolved
- [ ] Soft warnings (missing canonical, hollow, non-canonical) print but do not flip exit code
- [ ] Works when invoked from arbitrary CWD
- [ ] Fixture-based smoke test in `scripts/tests/test-verify.sh` covers pass + fail paths
close_reason: Implemented verify.sh + 19-test harness covering pass/fail/no-short-circuit/soft-warnings/arbitrary-CWD. All hard rules met. Discovered a pre-existing bug in claude-md.sh (missing-trailing-newline handling) — filed as separate bug per session rules.

title: validate-routes.py: resolve @-imports and markdown links in CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md
description:
## Description

Python script (stdlib only) that implements Phase 7 checks 1, 2, 5, 6 from `references/project-docs-lifecycle.md`: parse `CLAUDE.md` and `AGENTS.md` (and optionally docs), resolve every `@path` import and markdown link, and report unresolved references.

## Files to Create

- `plugins/project-docs/skills/project-docs/scripts/validate-routes.py` (executable, `#!/usr/bin/env python3`, stdlib only)
- `plugins/project-docs/skills/project-docs/scripts/tests/test-validate-routes.sh` (fixture-based smoke test covering pass + fail paths)

## Instructions

1. CLI shape:
   - `validate-routes.py <repo-root>` — validate `CLAUDE.md` + `AGENTS.md`
   - `--include-docs` optional flag to also validate links inside `docs/*.md`
2. Reference types to detect and resolve:
   - **`@path/to/file` import directives.** Only recognized when `@` is the first non-whitespace character on a line AND the line is **not** inside a fenced code block (```` ``` ```` or `~~~`) AND the `@` is not inside an inline-code span (backticks). Anywhere else, ignore. (Pinned per grill Q6 — without this, prose containing emails like `user@example.com` or URLs produce phantom errors. This repo's own AGENTS.md is a real example.)
   - Markdown inline links: `[text](path)` and `[text](path#anchor)`. Skip when inside a fenced code block.
   - Markdown reference links: `[text][ref]` + `[ref]: path`. Skip when inside a fenced code block.
   - Ignore: external URLs (`http://`, `https://`, `mailto:`)
3. Resolution rules:
   - Relative paths resolve against the file containing the reference
   - File-existence check is mandatory
   - **Anchor algorithm: GitHub-style** (pinned per grill Q5):
     - lowercase the heading text
     - replace spaces with `-`
     - strip characters that are not alphanumeric, `-`, or `_`
     - duplicate heading slugs get `-1`, `-2`, … suffixes in document order
   - Document the algorithm in `scripts/README.md` so reviewers can confirm parity with what GitHub renders
   - Skill references like `plugin-name:skill-name` (e.g. `beads-tasks:beads-core`) are NOT resolved against filesystem; treat as opaque and skip. **This is a known gap** — document in `scripts/README.md` as v1 limitation. (Per grill Q12.)
4. Output:
   - Default: human-readable list of unresolved references with `<file>:<line>: <ref>` format
   - `--json`: structured output `{unresolved: [{source_file, line, ref, kind, reason}], summary: {checked: N, unresolved: N}}`
5. Exit 0 if all resolve, non-zero if any unresolved

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Script exists and is executable
- [ ] Resolves `@AGENTS.md` import in `CLAUDE.md`
- [ ] Resolves all relative markdown links in `AGENTS.md`
- [ ] Detects broken links (intentional fixture with `[bad](does/not/exist.md)`) and exits non-zero
- [ ] Anchor validation catches `[link](file.md#nonexistent-heading)`
- [ ] Anchor validation succeeds for duplicate heading slugs (`-1`, `-2` suffixes) per GitHub algorithm
- [ ] **Ignores `@` inside fenced code blocks** — fixture: a ```` ``` ```` block containing `@made-up.md` must not be flagged
- [ ] **Ignores `@` inside inline code** — fixture: a line containing `` `@made-up.md` `` must not be flagged
- [ ] **Ignores `@` in prose** — fixture: a line containing `email user@example.com here` must not be flagged
- [ ] Skips external URLs and `plugin:skill` opaque refs without flagging
- [ ] `--include-docs` extends validation to `docs/*.md`
- [ ] `scripts/tests/test-validate-routes.sh` covers pass + fail paths and all "ignores" cases above
close_reason: Implemented validate-routes.py + 20-case test harness. GitHub anchor algorithm with duplicate-suffix handling. Code-fence/inline-code/prose @-ignore all verified. Skill refs (plugin:skill) opaque-skipped, documented in scripts/README.md (created here; wire-in task will extend). Real-repo run: 7 refs in root, 10 with --include-docs, all resolve. All acceptance criteria met.

title: inventory.py: structured doc inventory of canonical and non-canonical docs
description:
## Description

Python script (stdlib only) that performs Phase 1 (Inspect) of `references/project-docs-lifecycle.md` in one call. Emits structured JSON the agent (or `verify.sh`) can consume without re-doing filesystem discovery.

## Files to Create

- `plugins/project-docs/skills/project-docs/scripts/inventory.py` (executable, `#!/usr/bin/env python3`, stdlib only)
- `plugins/project-docs/skills/project-docs/scripts/tests/test-inventory.sh` (fixture-based smoke test)
- Optional: `scripts/_canonical.py` module if reused by `validate-routes.py`

## Instructions

1. CLI shape:
   - `inventory.py <repo-root>` — prints JSON to stdout
   - `--format=text` optional human-readable mode
2. Canonical doc set (from `references/project-setup.md`):
   - Root: `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`
   - `docs/`: `OVERVIEW.md`, `CODING.md`, `TESTING.md`, `RELEASING.md`, `MONITORING.md`, `CHANGE-WORKFLOW.md`
3. JSON output fields:
   - `canonical`: object keyed by canonical filename → `{present: bool, path: str|null, lines: int|null, non_heading_lines: int|null}`
   - `non_canonical_docs`: list of `{path, lines, non_heading_lines}` for any `docs/*.md` not in the canonical set (consolidation candidates)
   - `non_canonical_subdirs`: list of subdirectory paths inside `docs/` (e.g., `docs/adr/`, `docs/api/`). `references/project-structure.md` requires a flat `docs/` layout; subdirs are structural violations and must be surfaced. (Pinned per grill Q9.)
   - `location_violations`: list of `{file, found_at, expected_at}` for canonical docs found in the wrong place
   - `summary`: counts (canonical_present, canonical_missing, non_canonical_count, non_canonical_subdir_count, violation_count)
4. **No hollow heuristic, no threshold flag.** Emit raw `lines` and `non_heading_lines` counts only; let the agent or human decide what counts as hollow. (Pinned per grill Q4 — a boolean flag with no grounded threshold either floods Phase 7 with false positives or hides real hollow docs, and pointer-only files like AGENTS.md would mis-flag.)
   - `non_heading_lines` = count of non-blank lines whose stripped form does not start with `#`
5. Exit 0 on success; exit non-zero only on bad invocation (no `<repo-root>`, unreadable dir)
6. Walk `docs/` non-recursively for files (canonical taxonomy is flat); enumerate subdirectories separately to populate `non_canonical_subdirs`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Script exists and is executable
- [ ] Emits valid JSON for a fixture repo with mixed canonical + non-canonical docs
- [ ] Correctly flags missing canonical docs
- [ ] Correctly lists non-canonical `docs/*.md` files
- [ ] Correctly lists non-canonical subdirectories under `docs/`
- [ ] Detects canonical doc in wrong location (e.g., `OVERVIEW.md` at root)
- [ ] `non_heading_lines` count is correct for a fixture file with mixed headings/content
- [ ] `--format=text` produces readable output for the same fixture
- [ ] `scripts/tests/test-inventory.sh` runs against fixtures and passes
close_reason: Implemented inventory.py (executable, stdlib-only) + 24-case test harness. Emits raw lines/non_heading_lines counts (no boolean hollow flag per grill Q4). Real-repo run surfaces MONITORING.md missing + 2 non-canonical subdirs (docs/feedback/, docs/rules/). All acceptance criteria met.

title: claude-md.sh: create/repair CLAUDE.md and validate first-line invariant
description:
## Description

Bash script that handles the CLAUDE.md create/repair rule from `references/project-setup.md` and `references/docs-init.md` step 5: ensure `CLAUDE.md` exists at the project root with `@AGENTS.md` as the first non-empty line, preserving any existing content below.

## Files to Create

- `plugins/project-docs/skills/project-docs/scripts/claude-md.sh` (executable, `#!/usr/bin/env bash`, `set -euo pipefail`)
- `plugins/project-docs/skills/project-docs/scripts/tests/test-claude-md.sh` (executable test harness)

## Instructions

1. CLI shape:
   - `claude-md.sh init <repo-root>` — create/repair
   - `claude-md.sh check <repo-root>` — validate; exit 0 if OK, non-zero if missing or first non-empty line is not `@AGENTS.md`
2. `init` behavior covers exactly three states:
   - **Missing:** create `<repo-root>/CLAUDE.md` containing `@AGENTS.md` followed by a trailing newline
   - **Exists, first non-empty line is `@AGENTS.md`:** no change, exit 0
   - **Exists without `@AGENTS.md` as first non-empty line:** prepend `@AGENTS.md` + blank line; preserve all existing content unchanged below
3. **Never delete or move existing lines.** If `@AGENTS.md` already appears anywhere in the file but not as the first non-empty line, still prepend a new `@AGENTS.md` at the top. Claude Code de-dupes imports at load time; line-removal/move logic is fragile and out of scope. (Pinned per grill Q8.)
4. `check` performs the same first-non-empty-line check without writing
5. Treat lines containing only whitespace as empty for the "first non-empty line" check
6. **Atomic write contract:** write to a temp file created with `mktemp` in the same directory as the target (so `mv` is atomic on the same filesystem), then `mv -f` over the original. Never write the target file in place.
7. Print a short human-readable summary on stdout for each action; errors on stderr

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Script exists and is executable
- [ ] `init` correctly handles all three states without data loss in the "exists without" case
- [ ] `init` against a file containing `@AGENTS.md` not at the top prepends a new line without removing or moving the existing occurrence
- [ ] `init` against a file whose first non-empty line is `@AGENTS.md` preceded by blank/whitespace-only lines exits 0 with no write
- [ ] `check` exits 0 on valid CLAUDE.md, non-zero on missing/malformed
- [ ] Atomic write used: temp file in same dir + `mv -f` (no torn writes if interrupted)
- [ ] `scripts/tests/test-claude-md.sh` covers: missing file, already-correct, exists-with-content-only, exists-with-AGENTS-in-middle, exists-with-leading-whitespace-then-AGENTS, check-pass, check-fail; running it passes
close_reason: Implemented claude-md.sh init|check + 12-case test harness. Atomic-write via mktemp+mv -f in same dir. Duplicate-@AGENTS.md case verified: prepends without removing existing occurrence. All acceptance criteria met. Changes uncommitted per session instructions.

title: project-docs: deterministic scripts (bash + python)
description:
## Description

Add a small set of deterministic scripts to the `project-docs` skill at `plugins/project-docs/skills/project-docs/scripts/`. The scripts handle the mechanical parts of the docs lifecycle (CLAUDE.md repair, inventory, route validation, verification) so skill flows call them instead of re-discovering state via agent reads.

## Motivation

Today the agent performs many `ls`/`Read` calls during Phase 1 (Inspect) and Phase 7 (Verify) of `references/project-docs-lifecycle.md`. The rules — `@AGENTS.md` first-line invariant, canonical doc taxonomy, route resolution — are crisp enough to encode in scripts. Scripts also give us a single command users can run to validate docs without an agent in the loop.

## Design

**Runtime split:**

- **Bash** for thin file operations and the orchestrator: `claude-md.sh`, `verify.sh`.
- **Python (stdlib only, no deps)** for parsers and structured output: `inventory.py`, `validate-routes.py`.

No new runtime dependencies. Python 3 stdlib only.

**Script set (first cut):**

| Script | Purpose | Maps to |
|---|---|---|
| `claude-md.sh init\|check` | Create/repair CLAUDE.md so `@AGENTS.md` is the first non-empty line; preserve content below | docs-init step 5, docs-update step 2, lifecycle Phase 7 check 4 |
| `inventory.py` | Walk repo, classify against canonical doc set, JSON output | lifecycle Phase 1 (Inspect) |
| `validate-routes.py` | Resolve `@path` imports and markdown links in `CLAUDE.md` + `AGENTS.md`; report unresolved | lifecycle Phase 7 checks 1, 2, 5, 6 |
| `verify.sh` | Orchestrator: runs claude-md check + validate-routes + hollow-doc flag from inventory; non-zero on hard failures | lifecycle Phase 7 (mandatory) |

**Wiring:** After scripts ship, update `references/docs-init.md`, `references/docs-update.md`, `references/project-docs-lifecycle.md`, and `references/project-doc-review-guidelines.md` to call the scripts at the relevant phase steps.

## Out of scope (follow-ups, file separately if wanted)

- `scaffold-agents` / `scaffold-doc <topic>` generators
- `find-hollow-docs` as a standalone CLI
- `size-report` for Phase 5 slim/split heuristics
- Skill ↔ doc overlap detector

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All four scripts exist at `plugins/project-docs/skills/project-docs/scripts/` and are executable
- [ ] `claude-md.sh` handles all three states (missing / correct / exists-without)
- [ ] `inventory.py` emits valid JSON covering canonical doc presence, non-canonical docs, line counts, location violations
- [ ] `validate-routes.py` resolves links/imports and exits non-zero on unresolved refs
- [ ] `verify.sh` aggregates exit codes and prints a readable summary
- [ ] Skill reference docs call the scripts at the right lifecycle phases
- [ ] `scripts/README.md` documents the CLI surface
- [ ] Acceptance review task closed
close_reason: Epic complete. All 6 children closed (dtc claude-md.sh, 62i inventory.py, qmd validate-routes.py, 69b verify.sh, 0it bug-fix discovered during 69b, 3yq wire-in). Acceptance review 8q1 APPROVED with full evidence. Four scripts ship at plugins/project-docs/skills/project-docs/scripts/ with 77 passing test assertions and an aggregating run-all.sh runner. verify.sh exits 0 against marketplace root and examples fixture. Reference docs (docs-init, docs-update, project-docs-lifecycle, project-doc-review-guidelines) wired to call scripts at the right phase steps. Changes uncommitted per user instruction.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
## Plan-review grill (reviewer agent, applied by orchestrator without user walkthrough per user instruction "do not ask questions, just do the work")

13 findings: 5 BLOCKER, 5 SIGNIFICANT, 3 NIT. Decisions below.

### Accepted (task updates applied)

- **Q3 (SIGNIFICANT)** — Drop the "python3 fallback paragraph" requirement from task 3yq. No documented platform target lacks python3; carrying prose fallback re-introduces what the scripts replace.
- **Q4 (BLOCKER)** — Drop the boolean `hollow` flag from `inventory.py` output and the `--hollow-threshold` flag. Emit raw `lines` and `non_heading_lines` counts; let the agent/human classify. Pointer-only files like AGENTS.md must not auto-flag.
- **Q5 (BLOCKER)** — Pin anchor-slug algorithm in `validate-routes.py` to GitHub's (lowercase, spaces→`-`, strip non-alphanumeric except `-_`, duplicate headings get `-1`/`-2`/… suffix). Document in `scripts/README.md`.
- **Q6 (BLOCKER)** — `validate-routes.py` @-import parser must ignore `@` inside fenced code blocks and inline `code` spans. Add fixture acceptance criterion.
- **Q7 (BLOCKER)** — Drop AR criterion "verify.sh against `plugins/project-docs/` exits 0" — that directory isn't a project root, it's a skill payload. Replace with verify against marketplace root and against the `examples/` fixture under the skill.
- **Q8 (SIGNIFICANT)** — Pin `claude-md.sh init` rule: "if `@AGENTS.md` already appears anywhere in the file but not as first non-empty line, prepend anyway; never delete or move existing lines." Claude Code de-dupes imports at load time.
- **Q9 (SIGNIFICANT)** — `inventory.py` flags non-canonical subdirs of `docs/` via `non_canonical_subdirs` field. Project-structure rule requires flat docs/ layout.
- **Q10 (SIGNIFICANT)** — AR criterion "no regressions" replaced with concrete before/after diffs per modified reference doc pasted into the AR comment.
- **Q11 (SIGNIFICANT)** — Add `scripts/tests/run-all.sh` to task 3yq instructions. Test scripts that aren't run together rot.
- **Q12 (NIT)** — Accept "opaque skip" for skill refs (`plugin:skill`) in v1; document as a known gap in `scripts/README.md`. File follow-up if it bites.

### Rejected

- **Q1 (BLOCKER) — "cut to 2 scripts"** — REJECT. User explicitly confirmed the 4-script plan. Small composable scripts are easier to test than one orchestrator-with-everything. Each script maps to one lifecycle concern.
- **Q2 (SIGNIFICANT) — "all python"** — REJECT. User explicitly said "we create bash and python script". Mitigation: tighten `claude-md.sh` atomic-write spec (mktemp in same dir + `mv`); cover whitespace-only "empty" line edge case in tests.
- **Q13 (NIT) — "spec-shake checkpoint"** — REJECT. The natural workflow has the wire-in author reading the scripts before editing — that IS the checkpoint. If mismatch found, file a discovered bug per orchestration rules.

### Net scope: unchanged. Five impl tasks + AR. Spec sharpened in dtc, 62i, qmd, 3yq, 8q1.

title: Add internal-consistency validation: section-level cross-refs and version mirrors
description:
## Description

Two recent slips share the same root cause: internal references and version mirrors drift silently because nothing validates them.

1. `planning.md:9` referenced "the Decision Framework in core-rules.md" — section did not exist (broken since commit 3705170). Already fixed.
2. `marketplace.json` plugin versions drifted out of sync with `plugin.json` versions for all 5 plugins. Already fixed under closed dependency claude-dev-tools-5kg.

Neither class of bug is caught by automation. `plugin-dev:plugin-validator` is an external agent (ships in anthropics/claude-code, not this repo) and only checks file-level markdown links; the release process only bumps `plugin.json`. This task adds automated, mechanically-enforced checks for both classes.

## Instructions

(Approach revised by plan-review gate — see comments.)

1. Create one in-repo Python script `scripts/check-internal-consistency.py` running BOTH checks under a single non-zero-on-failure exit code:
   a. **Section-reference check** — scan markdown for the anchored pattern `<Phrase> section in <file>.md` (and reverse order). For each match, resolve <Phrase> against the headings of <file>.md using the GitHub heading-slug algorithm imported from `plugins/project-docs/skills/project-docs/scripts/validate-routes.py` (do not copy it), plus a case-insensitive substring fallback. Unresolved reference => FAIL. Skip fenced/inline code and any `examples/` or `notes/` directories.
   b. **Version-mirror check** — compare each `plugins/*/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` version against its entry in `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json`; any mismatch => FAIL. Reuse the comparison logic from the docs/RELEASING.md step-3 version-diff one-liner.
2. Wire the script into the required test gate: add `tests/marketplace/script-tests/run-all.sh` (discovered unchanged by `tests/run-all.sh`) that invokes the script and committed negative-test fixtures.
3. Add committed fixture-based negative tests: one desyncing a fixture plugin.json version, one with a bogus `<Phrase> section in <file>.md` reference to a nonexistent section — each asserting non-zero exit. Follow the `tests/project-docs/script-tests` pattern.
4. Update `docs/RELEASING.md` to note both checks are covered by the `tests/run-all.sh` gate (not a separate manual step).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `scripts/check-internal-consistency.py` exits 0 on current `main` as-is, and the section-reference scanner resolves at least one real reference (proving it is not a no-op)
- [ ] Deliberately desyncing one plugin.json version produces a non-zero exit (covered by a committed fixture test)
- [ ] Adding a `<Phrase> section in <file>.md` pointer to a nonexistent section produces a non-zero exit (covered by a committed fixture test)
- [ ] `tests/marketplace/script-tests/run-all.sh` is discovered and run by `tests/run-all.sh`, and a check failure fails that gate
- [ ] `docs/RELEASING.md` reflects that the checks run under the test gate
close_reason: Implemented and verified — all 5 acceptance criteria pass with independent test execution. Follow-up: claude-dev-tools-67b.

comments.author: Hans Kohlreiter
comments.text:
Plan-review gate (reviewer grill sheet) resolved with user:

- Q1/Q2 [APPROACH]: 'extend plugin-validator' is INFEASIBLE — plugin-dev:plugin-validator ships in the external anthropics/claude-code marketplace, not this repo. Decision: build one in-repo standalone Python script (scripts/check-internal-consistency.py), both checks under a single exit code, importing the GitHub-slug algorithm from plugins/project-docs/skills/project-docs/scripts/validate-routes.py rather than copying it.
- Q7/Q8 [ENFORCEMENT]: a manual plugin-validator step does not mechanically gate a release. Decision: wire the script into the existing required test gate via tests/marketplace/script-tests/run-all.sh (picked up unchanged by tests/run-all.sh discovery, find -mindepth 3 -maxdepth 3).
- Q5 [WARN vs FAIL]: instructions step 1 said 'warn'; acceptance criterion #3 says 'fail'. Criterion #3 is binding — unresolved section references FAIL, no warn-only mode.
- Q3/Q4/Q11 [SCANNER SCOPE]: scan only an anchored pattern ('<Phrase> section in <file>.md' and reverse order); resolve against GitHub heading-slugs plus case-insensitive substring match; skip fenced/inline code and any examples/ and notes/ directories.
- Q6 [VERSION CHECK]: promote the existing docs/RELEASING.md step-3 version-diff one-liner into the script; do not reinvent comparison logic.
- Q9 [CRITERION #1]: 'after fixing bd-12 and the Decision Framework bug' preconditions are already done (dep 5kg CLOSED, versions all 1.10.0, no 'Decision Framework' string in repo). Restated as 'exits 0 on current main'; scanner must also resolve >=1 real reference so a no-op scanner cannot pass.
- Q10 [TESTS]: criteria #2/#3 covered by committed fixture-based negative tests, not one-time manual checks.
- Q12 [RUNTIME]: Python, single file.

Description and acceptance criteria updated accordingly. need:review cleared.

title: Acceptance Review: keep-awake-linux plugin
description:
## Description

Gate task for closing epic claude-dev-tools-yt7. Runs after all child implementation tasks (claude-dev-tools-0vo, claude-dev-tools-8ed, claude-dev-tools-guz, claude-dev-tools-h97) are closed. Verifies the plugin works end-to-end on the user's real Linux desktop using observation-only methods. NO suspend or hibernate commands are ever executed during required verification.

## Forbidden commands during required verification

```
systemctl suspend
systemctl hibernate
systemctl poweroff
systemctl reboot
loginctl suspend
loginctl hibernate
```

All required verification uses `systemd-inhibit --list` (read-only) and `keep-awake status`. We test that the inhibitor is *registered with logind*, not that the OS subsequently honors it — logind's documented behavior is not under test here.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All child tasks closed (claude-dev-tools-0vo, claude-dev-tools-8ed, claude-dev-tools-guz, claude-dev-tools-h97).
- [ ] Plugin installs cleanly via `/plugin install keep-awake-linux@claude-dev-tools` with no errors.
- [ ] No manual `~/.claude/settings.json` edits required to enable hooks.

### Core lifecycle

- [ ] **Scenario 1 (cold → busy)**: from empty state, start a Claude session → within ~1s `systemd-inhibit --list` shows a `claude-keep-awake` entry; `keep-awake status` lists the session.
- [ ] **Scenario 2 (refresh on activity)**: during the session, submit a prompt, watch Claude use tools, then submit another prompt → throughout, `systemd-inhibit --list` continues to show the entry (TTL kept fresh by PreToolUse/PostToolUse/UserPromptSubmit).
- [ ] **Scenario 3 (released via `SessionEnd`)**: exit the Claude session cleanly → within ~1s the inhibitor releases.

### Multi-session

- [ ] **Scenario 4 (parallel sessions)**: with two Claude sessions running, `systemd-inhibit --list | grep -c claude-keep-awake` = 2.
